suite.name: VC Frontend Application (2.X.X)
suite.description:
Switch to storefront less
For Frontend application from 2.X.X need to set a new configuration in environment.yaml
storefront: enabled: false
routes: - host: vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com root: B2B-store paths: - path: /xapi route: platform - path: /files route: platform - path: /connect/token route: platform - path: /graphql route: platform - path: /revoke/token route: platform - path: /api/files route: platform - path: /externalsignin route: platform - path: /signin-oidc route: platform - path: /signin-google route: platform
---
This settings should be set up: For correct registration: Admin > Store > B2B > Store URL > add store url For correct URL: Admin > Store > B2B > Settings > toggle ON for Is SPA, SEO links-Long
1 - If in the title of the test-cases block you will find "Desktop + Mobile" - it means same test-cases for desktop and mobile. 2 - If in the title of the test-cases block you will NOT find "Desktop + Mobile" - it means different test-cases for desktop and mobile. So it will be next: 2.1. All test-case in the block will be for desktop. 2.2. The test-cases with title "MOBILE ..." - will be for mobile only.

case.title: Valid path > Category exists > Status code 200
Invalid path > URL/aaa/category1/subcategory1 > Status code 404
Valid path > Product exists > Status code 200
Invalid path > URL/404/category/subcategory/product_slug > Status code 404
Valid path > Static content exists > Status code 200
Invalid path > Static content does not exists > Status code 404
Invalid path > URL/aaa/Page > Status code 404
Registration form > PERSONAL> general view

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store anonymously.

step.content: 1. Click the link from the header Registration
step.expected: 1. User redirect to the registration page 2. Registration form opens

step.content: 1. Open home page. 2. On sigh in form click button Register
step.expected: 1. User redirect to the registration page 2. Registration form opens

step.content: 1. Open the Sign-in page from the header. 2. On sigh in form click button Register
step.expected: 1. User redirect to the registration page 2. Registration form opens

step.content: 1. Check the register form page.
step.expected: 1. Next elements are presented: - Header is the same as on usual pages for Anonymous User. - Footer is the same as on usual pages. - Registration form

step.content: 1. Check the Registration form
step.expected: 1. The title Registration 2. Switcher between Personal and Organization( selected by default) accounts 3. Registration form contains next fields: - First name (required) - Last name (required) - Email (required) - Password (required) with eye icon - Confirm password (required) with eye icon 4. Button Register 5. Tips for creating a strong password

case.title: Create account > PERSONAL user

step.content: 1. Enter necessary information: - First name - Last name - Email - Password - Confirm password 2. Click the button Sign up (enabled by default)
step.expected: 1. The page with the text "Registration successfully completed" 2. "Home page" button

step.content: 1. Click on the button Home page
step.expected: 1. Home page opens.

step.content: 1. Check the mail
step.expected: 1. Mail that Registration was success received

step.content: 1. Try to log in as User_1 using email
step.expected: 1. User_1 logged in store

step.content: 1. Go to Admin Environment > Contacts 2. Check the Contact_1
step.expected: 1. Contact_1 created

step.content: 1. Go to Admin Environment > Security > Users 2. Check the User_1 3. Check account type
step.expected: 1. User_1 created 2. Account type is **Customer**

case.title: Registration form > Password visibility

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store Anonymously

step.content: 1. Open Personal registration form 2. Fill __Password__ + __Confirm password__ fields 3. Pay attention on the filling passwords symbols and Eye icons states
step.expected: 1. Password symbols invisible 2. Eye icons is not crossed out

step.content: 1. Click on the each Eye icon 2. Pay attention to the Password symbols and Eye icon state
step.expected: 1. Password symbols are visible 2. Eye icons is crossed out

step.content: 1. Click on the each Eye icon one more time 2. Pay attention to the Password symbols and Eye icon state
step.expected: 1. Password symbols invisible 2. Eye icons is not crossed out

step.content: 1. Insert hide symbols in the password field by CTRL + A or other way 2. Copy that by CTRL + C or other way 3. Paste it in the any field
step.expected: 1. Invisible password symbols not copied

step.content: 1. Make password symbols visible (Click on the Eye icon) 2. Insert this symbols by CTRL + A or other way 3. Paste it in the any field
step.expected: 1. Visible password symbols copied successful

step.content: 1. Open Organization registration form 2. Repeat steps 1-5
step.expected: 1. Expected results similar

case.title: Validation > email

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store anonymously 2. Open Registration form

step.content:
1. Enter necessary information:
-- First name -- Last name -- Email address: enter wrong data (for example without "@") -- Username -- Password 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about incorrect email.

case.title: Validation > password match

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store anonymously 2. Open Registration form

step.content:
1. Enter necessary information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password: fill in 2 different passwords 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about passwords not match each other.

case.title: Validation > password policy

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store anonymously 2. Open Registration form 3. The rules of the policy may depend. Check the list or the rules before.

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, Qwert1!)
2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about Password must be at least 8 characters.

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, qwerty1!) 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about Passwords must have at least one uppercase ('A'-'Z')

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, QWERTY1!) 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about Passwords must have at least one lowercase ('a'-'z')

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, Qwertyu!) 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about Passwords must have at least one digit ('0'-'9')

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, Qwerty12) 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about Passwords must have at least one non-alphanumeric character

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, 11111!!!) 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about: - Passwords must have at least one lowercase ('a'-'z') - Passwords must have at least one uppercase ('A'-'Z') - Passwords must have at least 3 unique characters

step.content:
1. Enter necessary Personal information:
- First name - Last name - Email address - Username - Password (for example, 1) 2. Click button Next step
step.expected: 1. Error message shows about: - Password must be at least 8 characters - Passwords must have at least one non-alphanumeric character - Passwords must have at least one lowercase ('a'-'z') - Passwords must have at least one uppercase ('A'-'Z') - Passwords must have at least 3 unique characters

case.title: Validation > error messages > localization

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store

step.content: 1. Choose localization EN or DE 2. Open registration form 3. Fill in the First name 4. Clean First name 5. Click away from the First name field 6. Pay attention on the error message
step.expected: 1. Error message is notified user in the language which was selected

step.content: 1. Fill the Username field (Username exist in the system) 2. Pay attention on the error message
step.expected: 1. Error message is notified user in the language which was selected

step.content: 1. Enter First name with more than 64 chars 2. Fill in the other necessary information 3. Pay attention on the error message
step.expected: 1. Error message is notified user in the language which was selected

case.title: User is anonymous > Token is invalid or expired > Resend link

custom.preconds:
Email address verification enabled = true (Admin > Store > Settings)
Email address verification required = false
The user signed up
Email_1 contains a confirmation letter with the link

step.content: Open Email_1 with an invalid token
step.expected:
The user sees the page "Email confirmation" and the button **Resend link**
In order to continue, you still need to finalize verification process. Please use the button below to request a new link

step.content: The user clicks on the “Resend link“ button
step.expected:
The user stays on the same page but with differences:
Text: We sent you the confirmation link to your inbox.
Button Homepage

step.content: Click on Home page
step.expected: The Home page is opened

step.content: Check the Email
step.expected: The Email Confirmation with a valid/fresh link is displayed in the inbox

step.content: Click the link
step.expected:
The successful email confirmation page is opened with sign in button
![](index.php?/attachments/get/adbf5244-7060-4089-8699-51e84eb2d4ac)

case.title: User is registered > Token is invalid or expired > Resend link

The user signed up

step.content: Sign in as a new user
step.expected: The user on the /account/dashboard page

The user sees the page "Email confirmation" and the button **Resend link**
In order to continue you still need to finalize verification process. Please use the button below to request a new link.

step.expected:

Text: “We sent you a email confirmation link to your email.“
Button “Homepage“

step.expected: The user on the /account/dashboard page

case.title: Code > user_is_temporary_locked_out > entered the wrong password 5 times in a row

step.content: Go to the Sign-in page Enter the wrong password 5 times in a row Sign in Check the query login > Preview (Devtools > Network)
step.expected:
**The error description is:**
Your account has been temporarily locked. Please try again after some time.
**Storefront message is:**
You have been blocked. You can not reach any information or create the order. For more details please contact your site administrator

case.title: Login > check /connect/token

step.content: Open dev tools > Network Sign in as a **company owner** (Account type = Manager) Check token request in Network
step.expected: The user is logged successfully The status code is 200 for token request

step.content: Open dev tools > Network Sign in as a **company member** (Account type = Customer) Check token request in Network
step.expected: The user is logged successfully The status code is 200 for token request

step.content: Open dev tools > Network Sign in under a **personal account** Check token request in Network
step.expected: The user is logged successfully The status code is 200 for token request

case.title: Login > Reload the page > check the token

custom.preconds:
Set setting in environments.yml for testing
Authorization__AccessTokenLifeTime: 0:00:30

step.content:
Log in
Close the tab
Open within 30 seconds
step.expected: The refresh token is passed to all queries(getStore, getMe, getShortCart) The user is not logged out

step.content: Log in > Open order details Stay 30 seconds on the page Refresh the page
step.expected: The refresh token is passed to all queries(getStore, getMe, getShortCart) The user is not logged out

case.title: Login as a User1 > log out > Login as a User2

custom.preconds:
Set setting in environments.yml for testing
Authorization__AccessTokenLifeTime: 0:00:30

step.content: Login as a User1 Open Orders > Order details Open devTools > Application > Remove auth in local storage
step.expected: The User is logged out The sign-in page is opened The Return URL is shown

step.content: Sign in as a User2
step.expected: The 403 page is opened

case.title: Account type is Manager > Log out > check revoke/token

step.expected: The user is logged successfully The status code is 200 for token request

step.content: Open dev tools > Network Sign out Check revoke/token
step.expected: The user is logged out successfully The status code is 200 for revoke/token request

step.expected: The user is logged successfully The status code is 200 for token request

step.content: Open dev tools > Network Sign out Check revoke/token
step.expected: The user is logged out successfully The status code is 200 for revoke/token request

step.expected:
User1 can log in only by his own account User1 sees 403 error page Access denied (/connect/token with grant-type: impersonate returned 403 forbidden)

case.title: Sign in page > Password visibility

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store Anonymously

step.content: 1. Open Sign in form (from home page or header link) 2. Fill __Password__ field 3. Pay attention on the filling password symbols and Eye icon state
step.expected: 1. Password symbols invisible 2. Eye icon is not crossed out

step.content: 1. Click on the Eye icon 2. Pay attention to the Password symbols and Eye icon state
step.expected: 1. Password symbols are visible 2. Eye icon is crossed out

step.content: 1. Click on the Eye icon one more time 2. Pay attention to the Password symbols and Eye icon state
step.expected: 1. Password symbols invisible 2. Eye icon is not crossed out

step.content: 1. Insert hide symbols in the password field by CTRL + A or other way 2. Copy that by CTRL + C or other way 3. Paste it in the Username field
step.expected: 1. Invisible password symbols not copied

step.content: 1. Make password symbols visible (Click on the Eye icon) 2. Insert this symbols by CTRL + A or other way 3. Paste it in the Username field
step.expected: 1. Visible password symbols copied successful

case.title: Main page > sigh in form > Enter 5 times wrong password > account is locked

custom.preconds: User_1 exists in the system

step.content: 1. Enter a valid Email 2. Try to log in with the wrong password 5 times 3. Check that the validation message shows the correct text
step.expected:
The user sees an error message:
“You have been blocked. You can not reach any information or create the order. For more details please contact the site administrator ”.

step.content: Try to log in with a valid email and password
step.expected: The user has been locked. An error message is displayed: “You have been blocked. You can not reach any information or create the order. For more details please contact the site administrator”.

case.title: Forgot password

custom.preconds: 1. User_1 created 2. Go to the store 3. Open sign-in form (Main page or Sign-in page)

step.content: 1. Click Forgot password link under Sign-in form
step.expected: 1. New page with reset password fields opens

step.content: 1. Check the page
step.expected: 1. Next elements are presented: - Title __Reset your password__ - Notification: We will send you an email with reset password instructions - Input e-Mail field (required) (e.g Mail_1 - existed) - Button Submit

step.content: 1. Enter mail of the User_1 2. Click the button Submit
step.expected: 3. Page opens with next presented element: - Title __Reset your password__ - Notification: We sent you a reset password link to your inbox. - Button Home page

step.content: 1. Go to the Mail_1 2. Check the mail
step.expected: 1. Mail with reset password link presented

step.content: 1. Open the link 2. Check the page
step.expected:
1. Next elements are presented:
- Title __Reset your password__ - Notification: Enter a new password - Password field(required) with eye icon - Confirm Password(required) with eye icon - Button Reset password - Tips for creating a strong password

step.content: 1. Go to Admin Environment> Open Notifications > Notifications activity feed 2. Open the last notification with a link 3. Open the link 4. Check the page
step.expected:
1. Next elements are presented:
- Title __Reset your password__ - Notification: Enter a new password - Password field(required) with eye icon - Confirm Password(required) with eye icon - Button Reset password - Tips for creating a strong password
![](index.php?/attachments/get/3d201956-3f18-484b-aa1c-8a94242e956d)

step.content: 1. Enter the password twice 2. Click button Reset password 3. Check the page
step.expected:
1. Next elements are presented - Title __Reset your password__ - Notification: Your password has been reset successfully
- Button Log in

step.content: 1. Click button Log in
step.expected: 1. Sign in form opens

step.content: 1. Try to login using a new password.
step.expected: 1. User_1 log in to the store 2. Dashboard page open

section.name: Sign-in under Organization account
section.description:
https://virtocommerce.atlassian.net/browse/VCST-2630
**Priority:**
1. Selected Organization - only if Selected Organization in Default company + Member of company.
2. Default Company
3. First org from Member of company
---
![](index.php?/attachments/get/1000004617)

case.title: Default Organization = Selected

custom.preconds: The customer has multiple organizations (OrgA, OrgB, etc.). Default company is set.

step.content:
1. Sign in to the frontend as a customer.
2. Verify that the customer is logged into the Default company.
3. Sign Out - Sign In
step.expected: - Customer is logged into the Default company.

case.title: Verify Organization Selection When Default Company Is Empty

custom.preconds: The customer has multiple organizations. Default company is empty. The customer is a member of at least one company.

step.content: 1. Sign in to the frontend as a customer. 2. Verify that the customer is logged into the first organization from the list of member companies.
step.expected: - Customer is logged into the first organization from the list of member companies.

case.title: Verify Organization Switching

custom.preconds: The customer is logged into a default or assigned organization.

step.content: 1. Switch to another organization (e.g., OrgB) from the frontend. 2. Sign Out 3. Sign In
step.expected: A customer Sign In under Last Selected OrgB

case.title: Verify Organization Persistence After Sign Out and Sign In on Another Browser

custom.preconds: The customer has switched to OrgB. Customer.SelectedOrganization is set to OrgB.

step.content: 1. Sign out from the application. 2. Open the application in another browser. 3. Sign in as the same customer. 4. Verify that the customer is logged into OrgB. 5. Check the API call to /token to ensure that the OrganizationId is OrgB.
step.expected: - The customer is assigned to OrgB upon signing in. - API call /token contains OrganizationId of OrgB. - Customer.SelectedOrganization retains OrgB.

case.title: Invite member > Assign multiple organization > Sign In under First org

custom.preconds: 1. Company owner invited a new member 2. Invited member completed Registration 3. Assign multiple organization for a new member (from admin site > Contacts) 4. Default company does not exist.

step.content: 1. Sign in as a customer. 2. Verify the priority for selecting an organization
step.expected: A new member has not a selected Organization, that is why the First organization from the Member list is returned.

case.title: Invite member > Assign multiple organization > Sign In under Default company

custom.preconds: 1. Company owner invited a new member 2. Invited member completed Registration 3. Assign multiple organization for a new member (from admin site > Contacts) 4. Set a Default company.

step.content: 1. Sign in as a customer. 2. Verify the priority for selecting an organization
step.expected: A new member sign in under Default Company

case.title: Customer User - Address List Consistency

custom.steps: 1. Log in to the storefront as kutasina.elena+user1@gmail.com / Password1! 2. Verify the shipping address dropdown is visible on top of the homepage. 3. Log in to the back end > go to Users > Search for the email. 4. Click 'Show contact' > Addresses. 5. Compare the list of addresses between the storefront and back end.
custom.expected: The shipping dropdown is displayed. Both address lists are exactly the same.

case.title: Organization User - Address List Consistency

custom.steps: 1. Log in to the storefront as kutasina.elena+user4@gmail.com / Password1! 2. Unfold the shipping address selector at the top of the homepage. 3. Go to back end > Contacts > B2B test organization > Addresses. 4. Compare the lists.
custom.expected: Shipping address list is displayed. The front end and back end address lists match.

case.title: Shipping Address Selection Persists in Cart

custom.steps: 1. Log in to the storefront as kutasina.elena+user1@gmail.com / Password1! 2. Select a shipping address from the header dropdown. 3. Go to the cart > shipping. 4. Verify the selected address appears as default. 5. Go back to homepage, select another address. 6. Navigate again to checkout > shipping.
custom.expected: Address from step 2 appears in cart shipping. After selecting a new address (step 5), it should appear preselected at shipping.

case.title: Cart Address Change Doesn’t Affect Header

custom.steps: Precondition: Complete previous test with same address in header and cart [C398789] 1. Go to checkout > shipping. 2. Change the shipping address. 3. Return to homepage.
custom.expected: Header address remains the same as in the precondition.

case.title: Backend Persistence of Address for Registered User

custom.steps: 1. Log in as kutasina.elena+user1@gmail.com / Password1!. 2. Select a shipping address from header dropdown. 3. In the back end, search for user > click 'Show contact'. 4. In dev tools, check network requests: - GET /api/members/{memberId} - Check selectedAddressId and the list of addresses.
custom.expected: selectedAddressId matches the ID of the address chosen in step 2.

case.title: Anonymous User - Address Stored in Local Storage

custom.steps: 1. Open the storefront homepage in Incognito/Anonymous mode. 2. Open the address selector > click Add New > fill the form > click Create. 3. Open dev tools > check local storage.
custom.expected: New address appears in header. New address is present in local storage.

case.title: Update data in profile > Selected address is not reset

step.content:
1. Click on Ship To
2. Select address in top header
3. Go to profile
4. Update currency/language
5. Take a look on the selected address in top header
step.expected: The Ship To address remain selected

1. Next elements are presented: Top section: - Search result title - Buttons View: Grid; List - Sort by drop-n-down menu
Filters section: - search - checkbox Show in stock - checkbox Available at branches - filters
Product blocks: - Product Title (clickable) - Item # - Image with "-N%" badge (if Sale price and List price differences more than 5%) - Check-box Compare; - N product in stock; - Price: sale price, list price; - Button Add to cart
Main section: - Search relegated products
Bottom section: - pagination - buttons View: Grid; List

section.name: Search in Category by Keyword
section.description:
https://virtocommerce.atlassian.net/browse/VCST-3355 Available from vc-frontend version 2.25.0
For a category page from builder io, you need to set the category ID, then the chip will be displayed

case.title: Placeholder text on category page

step.expected: Placeholder shows: `Search within {category name}` or chip `{Category}` is displayed

step.expected: Search popup shows results for “Paper” within “Craft” category only

step.expected:
Navigated to URL "/office-chairs?q=rotate" and results scoped to “office-chairs”
---

step.expected:

step.expected: Title: "Printers" is shown. The appropriate products are returned The Category url with keyword: /printers?q=epson Category title with Search for {Keyword} in {CaregoryName}

step.expected: Search resets to global. Results show products across all catalog

step.expected: User remains scoped to category, or chip is removed if function is toggled

step.expected: Chip updates to new category

case.title: Search by Pages

custom.preconds:
Settings for search by static content:
https://github.com/VirtoCommerce/vc-theme-b2b-vue/blob/demo/config/settings_data.json
"search_by_static_content_enabled": true

case.title: Search by Blogs
Verify that the Barcode Scanner button is accessible and activates on mobile

custom.preconds: User has a mobile device with a working camera. Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile browser. User has a valid barcode to scan.

step.content: 1. Open Virto Commerce Frontend on a mobile device. 2. Locate and click the 'Barcode Scanner' button. 3. Observe the camera permission prompt. 4. Select 'Allow' to grant camera access.
step.expected: The Barcode Scanner interface activates, and the camera view is displayed on the screen, ready to scan a barcode.

case.title: Verify that the Barcode Scanner button is accessible and activates on desktop

custom.preconds: User has a desktop with a working webcam. Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a desktop browser. User has a valid barcode to scan.

step.content: 1. Open Virto Commerce Frontend on a desktop browser. 2. Locate and click the 'Barcode Scanner' button. 3. Observe the camera permission prompt. 4. Select 'Allow' to grant camera access.
step.expected: The Barcode Scanner interface activates, and the webcam view is displayed on the screen, ready to scan a barcode.

case.title: Verify that the Barcode Scanner button is accessible and activates on tablet

custom.preconds: User has a tablet with a working camera. Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a tablet browser. User has a valid barcode to scan.

step.content: 1. Open Virto Commerce Frontend on a tablet. 2. Locate and click the 'Barcode Scanner' button. 3. Observe the camera permission prompt. 4. Select 'Allow' to grant camera access.
step.expected: The Barcode Scanner interface activates, and the camera view is displayed on the screen, ready to scan a barcode.

case.title: Verify that scanning a barcode redirects to a search page

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile device with Barcode Scanner activated. Camera permissions are granted. A barcode corresponding to a single product in the catalog is available.

step.content: 1. Point the camera at the barcode and scan it. 2. Wait for the system to process the barcode.
step.expected: The frontend redirects to search page associated with the scanned barcode.

case.title: Verify that scanning a barcode with multiple matches redirects to a search results page

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile device with Barcode Scanner activated. Camera permissions are granted. A barcode corresponding to multiple products in the catalog is available.

step.content: 1. Point the camera at the barcode and scan it. 2. Wait for the system to process the barcode.
step.expected: The frontend redirects to a search results page listing all products associated with the scanned barcode. The list displays relevant product details (e.g., name, thumbnail, price).

case.title: Verify that scanning an invalid or unrecognized barcode displays 'No Results Found'

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile device with Barcode Scanner activated. Camera permissions are granted. An invalid or unrecognized barcode (not in the catalog) is available.

step.content: 1. Point the camera at the invalid barcode and scan it. 2. Wait for the system to process the barcode.
step.expected: The frontend redirects to a 'No Results Found' page with an appropriate message (e.g., 'No products found').

case.title: Verify that denying camera permission prevents Barcode Scanner activation

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile device. User has not yet granted camera permissions.

step.content: 1. Locate and click the 'Barcode Scanner' button. 2. Observe the camera permission prompt. 3. Select 'Deny' to refuse camera access.
step.expected: The Barcode Scanner does not activate, and an error message or prompt is displayed (e.g., 'Camera access is required to use the Barcode Scanner').

case.title: Verify that the Barcode Scanner button is visible on Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is accessible on Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile devices.

step.content: 1. Open Virto Commerce Frontend on a Desktop browser. 2. Verify the 'Barcode Scanner' button is visible. 3. Open Virto Commerce Frontend on a Tablet browser. 4. Verify the 'Barcode Scanner' button is visible. 5. Open Virto Commerce Frontend on a Mobile browser. 6. Verify the 'Barcode Scanner' button is visible.
step.expected: The 'Barcode Scanner' button is visible and accessible on all specified devices.

case.title: Verify Barcode Scanner functionality under poor lighting conditions

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile device with Barcode Scanner activated. Camera permissions are granted. A valid barcode is available, and the environment has poor lighting (e.g., dim room).

step.content: 1. Point the camera at the barcode in poor lighting conditions and attempt to scan it. 2. Wait for the system to process the barcode.
step.expected: The Barcode Scanner successfully detects the barcode and redirects to the appropriate page (single product or search results), or provides feedback (e.g., 'Adjust lighting for better results') if it fails.

case.title: Verify Barcode Scanner behavior with a damaged or partially obscured barcode

custom.preconds: Virto Commerce Frontend is open on a mobile device with Barcode Scanner activated. Camera permissions are granted. A damaged or partially obscured barcode is available.

step.content: 1. Point the camera at the damaged barcode and attempt to scan it. 2. Wait for the system to process the barcode.
step.expected: The Barcode Scanner either successfully detects the barcode and redirects to the appropriate page or displays a 'No Results Found' page with an appropriate message (e.g., 'Unable to scan barcode').

case.title: Bulk order > general view

case.title: Bulk order > Manually > validation > quantity

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 - Product_7 exist in the store 2. Product_1 has SKU_1, Product_2 has SKU_2 etc. 3. Go to the store

step.content: 1. Open **BULK ORDER PAD** 2. Try to enter in the the block Copy&Paste next value: - SKU_1, quantity 5 - SKU_2, quantity 0 - SKU_3, quantity -1 - SKU_4, quantity 999 999 - SKU_5, quantity 100 0000 - SKU_6, quantity 2,3 - SKU_7, quantity leave empty 3. Click "Add to cart".
step.expected: 1. Product_2 - can not enter 0 in the field 2. Product_3 - can not enter -1 in the field 3. Product_5 - can not enter 100 000 in the field 4. Product_6 - can not enter 2,3 in the field 5. Only only positive, less than 999 999, only integer quantity possible enter to the field Quantity.

step.content: 1. Click "Add to cart".
step.expected: 1. Product_1 added to the cart in quantity 1 2. Product_4 added to the cart in quantity 999 999 3. Product_7 was not added to the cart

case.title: Bulk order > Copy&Pastу > validation

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 - Product_7 exist in the store 2. Product_1 has SKU_1, Product_2 has SKU_2 etc. 3. Go to the store

step.content: 1. Open **BULK ORDER PAD** 2. Try to enter in the the block Copy&Paste next value: - SKU_1, quantity 5 - SKU_2, quantity 0 - SKU_3, quantity -1 - SKU_4, quantity 999 999 - SKU_5, quantity 100 0000 - SKU_6, quantity 2,3 - SKU_7, quantity leave empty 3. Click "Add to cart".
step.expected: 1. Product_2 - can not enter 0 in the field 2. Product_3 - can not enter -1 in the field 3. Product_5 - can not enter 100 000 in the field 4. Product_6 - can not enter 2,3 in the field 5. Only only positive, less than 999 999, only integer quantity possible enter to the field Quantity.

step.content: 1. Click "Add to cart".
step.expected: 1. Product_1 added to the cart in quantity 1 2. Product_4 added to the cart in quantity 999 999 3. Product_7 was not added to the cart

case.title: Submit empty filed

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exist in the store 2. Product_1 has SKU_1 3. Go to the store

step.expected: 1. Button Add to cart is not active

step.content: 1. Open BULK ORDER PAD 2. Enter quantity as 1 3. Leave SKU field empty 4. Try to click button Add to cart
step.expected: 1. Button Add to cart is not active

case.title: Change Password > password policy

custom.preconds: 1. The rules of the policy may depend. Check the list or the rules before.

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, Qwert1!) - Confirm New Password (Qwert1!) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows error message 'Password must be at least 8 characters.'

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, qwerty1!) - Confirm New Password (qwerty1!) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows error message 'Passwords must have at least one uppercase ('A'-'Z').'

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, QWERTY1!) - Confirm New Password (QWERTY1!) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows error message 'Passwords must have at least one lowercase ('a'-'z').'

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, Qwertyu!) - Confirm New Password (Qwertyu!) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows error message 'Passwords must have at least one digit ('0'-'9').'

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, Qwerty12) - Confirm New Password (Qwerty12) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows error message 'Passwords must have at least one non-alphanumeric character.'

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, 11111!!!) - Confirm New Password (11111!!!) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows pull of Error messages: - Passwords must have at least one lowercase ('a'-'z') - Passwords must have at least one uppercase ('A'-'Z') - Passwords must have at least 3 unique characters

step.content: 1. Enter necessary Personal information: - First name - Last name - Old password - New Password (for example, 1) - Confirm New Password (1) 2. Click Update button
step.expected: 1. Shows pull of Error messages: - Password must be at least 8 characters - Passwords must have at least one non-alphanumeric character - Passwords must have at least one lowercase ('a'-'z') - Passwords must have at least one uppercase ('A'-'Z') - Passwords must have at least 3 unique characters

case.title: Orders > Pagination > Add pages

custom.preconds: Sign in as a company owner Create 10 orders

step.content: Open Orders tab View the page
step.expected: The Orders list is displayed without pagination

step.content: Create one order
step.expected:
1. The 2nd page has been added 2. Previous and Next buttons are displayed
**Buttons:**
Previous - active (disabled if the cursor is on the first page) Next - (active) disabled if the cursor is on the last page
**Pagination view:**
1 2

step.content: Generate 90 orders via Postman Check the number of pages
step.expected:
1. There are 9 pages for 10 Orders on each page
**Buttons:**
Previous - active/disabled if the cursor on the first page Next - active/disabled if the cursor on the last page
The 1st page is selected and active
**Pagination view:**
1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9

case.title: Orders > Pagination > Next button

custom.preconds: Generate 120 Orders via Postman Sign in as a company owner

step.content: Open Account > Orders tab Check Pagination
step.expected:
**Buttons:**
Previous - disabled if the cursor on the first page active Next - active
The 1st page is selected and active
There are 12 pages with 10 Orders on each
**Pagination view:**
1 2 3 4 5 6 7...12

step.content: Click Next button
step.expected:
1. System opens 2nd page > 2nd page is selected, pages in the list are changed 2. The page is scrolling up
**Buttons:**
Previous - active Next - active

step.content: Proceed to push the Next button till the 6-page
step.expected:
1. System opens 6th page > 6th page is selected, pages in the list are changed 2. The Previous and Next buttons are active 3. The page is scrolling up
Three dots after 1st and before the last page were added
**Pagination view:**
1...4 5 6 7 8...12

step.content: Proceed to push the Next button till the 8-page
step.expected:
1. System opens 8th page > 8th page is selected, pages in the list are changed 2. The Previous and Next buttons are active 3. The page is scrolling up
Three dots are displayed after the 1st page and all of the last seven pages.
**Pagination view:**
1...6 7 8 9 10 11 12

step.content: Proceed to push the Next button till the last page
step.expected:
1. System opens 12th page > 12th page is selected, pages in the list are changed 2. The page is scrolling up
Three dots are displayed after the 1st page and all of the last seven pages.
**Buttons:**
Previous - active/disabled if the cursor on the first page Next - active/disabled if the cursor on the last page
**Pagination view:**
1...6 7 8 9 10 11 12

case.title: Verify 'Agency' and 'Personal' Tabs Are Visible for Company Owner

custom.preconds: User is logged in as a company owner.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the Orders page. 2. Check if both 'Agency' and 'Personal' tabs are displayed.
custom.expected: Both tabs should be visible only for company owners.

case.title: Verify 'All orders' and 'My orders' Tabs Are Not Visible for Regular Users

custom.preconds: User is logged in as a regular organization user (not an owner).
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the Orders page. 2. Check if 'All orders' and 'My orders' tabs are displayed.
custom.expected: Regular users should not see 'All orders' and 'My orders' tabs.

case.title: Verify Order Access Based on User Role

custom.preconds: Orders exist for multiple users with different roles.
custom.steps: 1. Log in as a company owner. 2. Verify that both personal and agency orders are accessible. 3. Log in as a regular user. 4. Verify that only personal orders are visible.
custom.expected: Company owners should see all orders, while regular users should see only their own.

case.title: Verify 'Buyer Name' Column is Displayed

custom.preconds: User is logged in and navigates to the Order History page.
custom.steps: 1. Go to the Orders page. 2. Look at the order list table. 3. Check for the presence of the 'Buyer Name' column.
custom.expected: The 'Buyer Name' column should be visible and display correct names.

case.title: Verify 'All Orders' Tab Displays Orders

custom.preconds: User has multiple orders placed by different organization users.
custom.steps: 1. Go to the Orders page. 2. Select the 'Agency' tab. 3. Verify all organization users' orders are listed.
custom.expected: Orders should be visible under 'Agency' and include historical users.

case.title: Verify Filters Panel Opens

custom.preconds: User is on the Orders page.
custom.steps: 1. Click on the 'Filters' button. 2. Verify the filter panel appears.
custom.expected: Filters panel should appear with options for Create Date, Buyer name, Order Status, etc.

case.title: Verify Orders are Filtered by Date

custom.preconds: Orders exist with different creation dates.
custom.steps: 1. Open Filters panel. 2. Select a Start Date and End Date. 3. Click Apply and check the results.
custom.expected: Only orders within the selected date range should be displayed.

case.title: Verify Search Field is Present

custom.preconds: User is logged in and on the Orders page.
custom.steps: 1. Look for the search input field at the top of the Orders page.
custom.expected: Search field should be visible and allow input.

case.title: Verify Searching by Order Number

custom.preconds: Orders exist in the system.
custom.steps: 1. Enter an existing order number in the search field. 2. Press Enter or click the search button.
custom.expected: Only the order with the matching number should be displayed.

case.title: Verify Searching by Buyer Name

custom.preconds: Orders exist for different buyers.
custom.steps: 1. Click on filter > Buyer name > Select multiple members 2. Press Apply button.
custom.expected: Only orders matching the buyer name should be displayed.

case.title: Verify Pagination Appears

custom.preconds: More than 10 orders exist in the system.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the Orders page. 2. Check if pagination controls are visible.
custom.expected: Pagination should be available when more than 10 orders exist.

case.title: Verify Navigation Between Pages

custom.preconds: Pagination is present (more than 10 orders).
custom.steps: 1. Click on Next Page. 2. Verify the next set of orders is displayed. 3. Click on Previous Page and verify the previous orders return.
custom.expected: Orders list should update correctly when moving between pages.

case.title: Icon is grey > Remove the product from the wish list in the Grid view

step.expected: 1. List_1 moved to the 2nd block. 2. Check-box for the List_1 is unselected 3. The icon is grey

case.title: Icon is grey > Remove the product from the wish list in the List view

custom.preconds: 1. User_1 created 2. User_1 logged in to the store 3. User_1 has List_1-List_2 4. Product_1 exist in the store 5. Product_1 added to the List_1

step.content: 1. Go to category page > List view 2. Click on the colored heart icon 3. Click the button Add to the list 4. On the popup uncheck List_1 from the 1st block
step.expected: 1. List_1 moved to the 2nd block. 2. Check-box for the List_1 is unselected 3. The icon is grey

step.content: Switch to the Grid view Check the state of the icon
step.expected: The icon is also grey in the Grid view The product was removed from the wish list

case.title: Add/Remove product to wish list from category page > Open page in different Tabs

custom.preconds:
User_1 logged in to the store User_1 has List_1-List_2
Open the category page in 5 Tabs

step.content: Open Grid view Click on the Star icon on the Product1 card Select List1 > Save
step.expected: The product1 added to List1 The star became orange

step.content: Switch to the List view Check the state of the star
step.expected: The Star is also orange in the List view The product is on the wish list

step.content: Check the star in other Tabs in both the grid and list view
step.expected: The Star is also orange in all Tabs The product is on the wish list

step.content: Repeat all steps again for removing the product from the wish list

case.title: Print > 1 line-item
No limits > User enter value greater than available stock

The user opened a wish list

step.expected:
1. The validation error displays under the input field
**Max order 20 item(s)**
2. The Add to Cart button is disabled

step.content: Enter value less than available in stock (ex. 10)
step.expected: 1. There is no error message under the input field 2. The Add to Cart button is activated

step.content: 1. Set qty 10 > Add to cart 2. Set qty 21
step.expected:
1. The button changed to Update cart The warning message is displayed under the input
**Max order 20 item(s)**
2. The Update cart is disabled

case.title: Notifications > Pagination
Set up the firebase settings in environment.yml

custom.preconds:
Open environment.yml Set settings from firebase
name: string platform: config: PushMessages__UseFirebaseCloudMessaging: true PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__Type: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ProjectId: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__PrivateKeyId: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientEmail: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientId: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__AuthUri: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__TokenUri: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__AuthProviderX509CertUrl: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__ClientX509CertUrl: string PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__UniverseDomain: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__ApiKey: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__AuthDomain: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__ProjectId: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__StorageBucket: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__MessagingSenderId: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__AppId: string PushMessages__FcmReceiverOptions__VapidKey: string secretConfig: PushMessages__FcmSenderOptions__PrivateKey: firebase-private-key-new
custom.steps:
1. Open https://console.firebase.google.com/ Click Add project Enter project name Click Create project When the project is created, click Continue
2. Sender settings Click Project settings Click Service accounts Click Generate new private key In the popup window click Generate key Open the downloaded JSON file and copy all fields to the corresponding fields in the appSettings.json Copy private key value and put the resulting value in the Key Vault (Azure portal)
3. Receiver settings On the Project Overview page click Add Web app Enter application name Click Register app In the Add Firebase SDK section you will find settings that should be placed in the PushMessages.FcmReceiverOptions section in the appSettings.json Click Continue to console Click Project settings Click Cloud Messaging In the Web Push certificates section click Generate key pair
3. Save settings in environment.yml and push the pull request 4. Open the Frontend application > Console
custom.expected: The settings are set up successfully

case.title: Remove the Custom Organization Logo

custom.preconds: A custom organization logo has been successfully uploaded.

case.title: Update the Organization Logo

custom.preconds: A custom organization logo has been successfully uploaded.

step.content: 1. Navigate to the "Company Info" page. 2. Click on the "Browse your files" button in the Organization Logo block. 3. Select a new valid image file (JPG, PNG, GIF, less than 5MB). 4. Click the "Save" button. 5. Verify that the frontend calls the mutation to update the logo link. 6. Refresh the page and verify that the new logo is displayed.
step.expected: The new logo replaces the previous one

case.title: Invite member > Email > Delimiter

custom.preconds: Sign in as a company owner (role Organization maintainer) Open Account > Corporate > Company members

step.content: Click on Invite members Check Email field
step.expected:
The field is required and marked by asterisks
The title is:
Enter email addresses of people you want to invite*
The placeholder is:
Use commas, semicolons, or line breaks to separate multiple entries

step.content: Check email validator Start typing an email address
step.expected:
The red text error appears under the field on the fly:
Incorrect email addresses: new-email

step.content:
Enter several emails with space delimiter
rolfson.karli@johnston.info geovanni.weber@barrows.biz judah35@vandervort.biz alba34@gmail.com
step.expected: There is no error message. The field takes the space delimiter

step.content:
Enter several emails with semicolon delimiter
rolfson.karli@johnston.info; geovanni.weber@barrows.biz; judah35@vandervort.biz; alba34@gmail.com;
step.expected: There is no error message. The field takes the semi-colon delimiter

step.content:
Enter several emails with commas delimiter
rolfson.karli@johnston.info, geovanni.weber@barrows.biz, judah35@vandervort.biz, alba34@gmail.com,
step.expected: There is no error message. The field takes the commas delimiter

step.content:
Enter several emails with break line delimiter
rolfson.karli@johnston.info geovanni.weber@barrows.biz judah35@vandervort.biz alba34@gmail.com
step.expected: There is no error message. The field takes the break line delimiter

step.content:
Use mixed delimiters
rolfson.karli@johnston.info; geovanni.weber@barrows.biz, judah35@vandervort.biz, alba34@gmail.com,
step.expected: There is no error message. The field takes all valid delimiters

step.content:
Enter invalid delimiter (ex. | / % + - &)
rolfson.karli@johnston.info| geovanni.weber@barrows.biz/ judah35@vandervort.biz+ alba34@gmail.com&rolfson.karli@johnston.info; geovanni.weber@barrows.biz, judah35@vandervort.biz, alba34@gmail.com,
step.expected:
An error message appears below the field. The field does not accept invalid delimiters.

case.title: Tab Navigation - Moving Focus Forward

custom.preconds: The webpage contains multiple interactive elements (buttons, links, input fields, checkboxes, dropdowns, modals, etc.).

step.content: 1. Load the webpage. 2. Press the `Tab` key repeatedly. 3. Observe the focus movement.
step.expected: The focus moves sequentially to the next interactive element in a logical and predictable order. The focus indicator is visible. No elements are skipped.

case.title: Shift + Tab Navigation - Moving Focus Backward

step.content: 1. Load the webpage. 2. Navigate to the last interactive element using `Tab`. 3. Press `Shift + Tab` repeatedly. 4. Observe the focus movement.
step.expected: The focus moves sequentially to the previous interactive element. The focus indicator is visible. No elements are skipped.

case.title: Enter Key - Activating Buttons and Links

step.content: 1. Load the webpage. 2. Use `Tab` to navigate to a button. 3. Press `Enter` and observe the action. 4. Use `Tab` to navigate to a link. 5. Press `Enter` and observe the navigation.
step.expected: The button performs its intended action (e.g., form submission, modal opening, etc.). The link navigates to the intended destination.

case.title: Spacebar - Selecting Checkboxes and Radio Buttons

step.content: 1. Load the webpage. 2. Use `Tab` to navigate to an unchecked checkbox. 3. Press `Space` and observe the selection. 4. Use `Tab` to navigate to a radio button. 5. Press `Space` and observe the selection.
step.expected: The checkbox toggles between checked and unchecked states. The radio button is selected and deselects other radio buttons in the same group.

case.title: Escape Key - Closing Dropdowns and Modals

step.content: 1. Load the webpage. 2. Open a dropdown menu using the keyboard or mouse. 3. Press `Esc` and observe the behavior. 4. Open a modal window. 5. Press `Esc` and observe the behavior.
step.expected: The dropdown menu should close. The modal should close without affecting other page elements.

case.title: Visual Focus Indicator

step.content: 1. Load the webpage. 2. Use `Tab` to navigate through all interactive elements.
step.expected: A clear focus outline is visible. The focus is not lost at any point. Custom styling does not remove the focus indicator.

case.title: Filter > variations (only)

custom.preconds:
There are Variation properties that can be set up both for the Main product and its Variation. For this Test-case, Variation Properties should be set up __only__ for the Variation, not for the Main Product. Better to check on the Carriage bolt Catalog.
1. Product_1 has Variation_1 2. Product_1 __does not have set up Variation Properties__ (Go to Admin BackEnd > Catalog > Product_1 > widget Properties > Variation property > There is should NOT be set up Variation Propety (for example FASTENER LENGTH) 3. Variation_1 __has set up Variation property__ (Go to Admin BackEnd > Catalog > Product_1 > widget Variations > Variation_1 > widget Properties > Variation property > There is should be set up Variation Propety (for example FASTENER LENGTH) > add any value (for example 1") 4. Filters include FASTENER LENGTH (Go to Admin BackEnd > Store > B2b store > Agregation properties widget > Add FASTENER LENGTH to the Right column. 5. Product_1 exists in the Catalog_1 (Carriage bolts)

step.content:
1. Go to the store.
2. Open Catalog from preconditions (Carriage bolts)
3. Find the FASTENER LENGTH filter.
4. Select value 1".
step.expected: 1. Product_1 __presented__ as this filter (FASTENER LENGTH: 1") corresponds to the properties which were set up for Variation_1 of the Product_1

step.content:
1. Repeat step 3 from the precondition and __remove__ or __edite__ Variation Property FASTENER LENGTH : 2" 2. Go to the store.
3. Open Catalog from preconditions (Carriage bolts)
4. Find the FASTENER LENGTH filter.
5. Select value 1".

130
- The discount percentage is calculated and displayed as a label on the corner

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > price N/A or 0

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > widget __Price__ > set up the price as 0 or remove price

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the price and button.
step.expected: 1. The price is __updated__ = 0 2. Inactive field and button with is shown 3. Product could not be added to the cart

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > can't be purchased

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Switch off toggle __Can be purchased__

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the button.
step.expected: 1. Inactive field and button with is shown 2. Product could not be added to the cart

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > visible

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Switch off toggle __Visible__

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the button.
step.expected: 1. Inactive field and button with is shown 2. Product could not be added to the cart

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > out of stock > FFC 0

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > widget __Fulfillment center__ > Set 0 for all available FFC

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the button.
step.expected: 1. Red label __Out is stock__ shown 2. Inactive field and button with is shown 3. Product could not be added to the cart

case.title: Product details page > URL prefix

section.description:
https://virtocommerce.atlassian.net/browse/VCST-1607
Requirements:
Feature available from VC Frontend version 2.4.0
Install the ElasticSearch8 with semantic search Set ElasticSearch8 as a primary provider in environment.yml Set settings in admin site for ElasticSearch8 Install the module https://github.com/VirtoCommerce/vc-module-x-recommend

step.expected: The Product details page is open successfully

case.title: Check Config product card > Open PDP
Search the configuration product > Open PDP
Add Configuration Section

custom.preconds: A configurable product exists in the catalog.
custom.steps:
1. Open the configurable product(ex. Bike) 2. Click on Associations widget 3. Add a new group name configuration section with the specified attributes.
- Name: "Bike Components" - Optional/Mandatory: Mandatory - Quantity: 2 - Add items - Select products
4. Save changes 5. Check Config product in Frontend
custom.expected: The configuration section is added with products

case.title: Configurable Product PDP > View

custom.preconds: #### **Preconditions**: 1. The configurable product page is accessible on the storefront. 2. The product configuration is properly set up with sections and options.

step.content: 1. **Navigate to the Configurable Product Page** - Open the product page (ex. Bed with Additional Options.) - Click the Create Your Own Configuration button
step.expected: 1. The configuration product page is opened successfully 2. The Create Your Own Configuration is displayed and scrolling the page to first section

step.content: 2. **Verify Page Structure** - Confirm the title CONFIGURE THE PARAMETERS is displayed at the top of the configuration section. - Check that sections (e.g., BED LINEN SET, MATTRESS,SOFT PILLOWS, BLANKETS) are displayed as accordions.
step.expected: The CONFIGURE THE PARAMETERS title and sections are displayed correctly.

step.content: 3. **Verify Default Section State** - Ensure that the first section is expanded by default. - Confirm other sections are collapsed.
step.expected: Sections expand and collapse as expected.

step.content: 4. **Verify Section Options** - In the section: - Verify that the following options are displayed with corresponding prices: - **Mattress cover Fitted sheet, light pink, 90x200 cm**: $20.00 - **Bed with Additional Options Configurable bed**: $299.00 - **None**: No cost. - Check that a radio button is displayed beside each option.
step.expected: Radio buttons for all options are visible, selectable, and reflect the appropriate prices. The total price updates dynamically based on selected options.

step.content: 5. **Select Options** - Select the Mattress cover Fitted sheet, light pink, 90x200 cm option. - Verify that the radio button is checked and the price updates to reflect the selection.
step.expected: Radio buttons for all options are visible, selectable, and reflect the appropriate prices. The total price updates dynamically based on selected options.

step.content: 6. **Verify Price Calculation** - Verify that the total price updates dynamically in the PRICE AND DELIVERY section based on selected options.
step.expected: The total price updates dynamically based on selected options.

step.content: 7. **Collapse and Expand Sections** - Collapse the BED LINEN SET section and verify that it collapses properly. - Expand another section (e.g., MATTRESS) and confirm it displays the correct placeholder text (e.g., Nothing selected).
step.expected: Sections expand and collapse as expected.

step.content: 8. **Verify "None" Option** - Select the None option in the BED LINEN SET section. - Verify that the price adjusts to exclude the cost of any items in this section.
step.expected: Selecting None removes the cost of items in that section.

step.content: 9. **Verify Stock and Add to Cart** [Draft. Skip] - Confirm the stock indicator (e.g., 12 in stock) updates dynamically based on selected options. - Click ADD TO CART and verify the selected configuration is included in the cart.
step.expected: The cart displays the selected configuration, options, and total price accurately.

case.title: Validate Mandatory and Optional Sections

custom.preconds: A configurable product exists with a configuration section
custom.steps: 1. Create mandatory and optional sections. *Section 1 (Mandatory): "Pedals" and "Seat."* *Section 2 (Optional): "Basket" and "None."* 2. Save changes and view the product configuration page.
custom.expected:
Mandatory sections have one pre-selected option. Optional sections have 'None' pre-selected by default.
*In the mandatory section, one option is pre-selected by default.* *In the optional section, "None" is pre-selected by default*

case.title: Select several sections > Check Price Calculation

custom.preconds: A configurable product with pricing data for each SKU exists.
custom.steps:
1. Add a configurable product to the cart with selected options.
-Option 1: Pedals ($10) -Option 2: Seat ($20) 2. Check the cart details for the product price.
custom.expected: The product price is the sum of the base product price and the selected options. options (e.g., $10 + $20 = $30).

step.content: 1. Open config product in Front 2. Select option 3. Check qty and totals
step.expected:
For each product configuration, verify the quantity is displayed correctly (e.g., "2"). -the subtotal for each configuration is calculated as (Price per item) x (Quantity). -the total cart subtotal matches the sum of all individual product subtotals.
!!Totals are recalculated. Main Price + Option total price

case.title: File section > Verify Configurable Product Display

custom.preconds: User is on the configurable product page.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Verify that the configuration options (e.g., size, color, material) are displayed. 3. Verify that each configuration option has a set of valid choices.
custom.expected: - The product displays all configurable options and is ready for selection. - Each option has a list of valid choices.

case.title: File section > Verify File Section Availability

custom.preconds: User is on the configurable product page.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the product page with a file upload section. 2. Verify that the file upload section is visible on the page. 3. Check the instructions for uploading files.
custom.expected: - The file upload section is visible and properly placed on the page. - Instructions are clear for the user to upload files.

case.title: File section > Verify File Upload Functionality

custom.preconds: The file upload section is available.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Select a valid file to upload (e.g., PDF, DOCX, or image file depending on requirements). 3. Click the 'Upload' button to upload the file.
custom.expected: - The file uploads successfully without any error. - The uploaded file appears in the 'Uploaded Files' section or similar.

case.title: File section > Verify Invalid File Upload

custom.preconds: User is on the configurable product page with the file section.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Attempt to upload an invalid file (e.g., .exe or a file type not supported). 3. Attempt to upload a file that exceeds the size limit.
custom.expected: - An error message is displayed stating that the file is invalid or exceeds the size limit. - The file is not uploaded.

case.title: File section > Verify File Type Restrictions

custom.preconds: The file upload section should allow specific file types (e.g., PDF, image files).
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Try uploading a file of an unsupported type (e.g., .exe, .bat). 3. Check the behavior.
custom.expected: - The site should not allow uploading of unsupported file types. - An error message should appear indicating the allowed file types (e.g., 'Only PDF, PNG, and JPG files are allowed').

case.title: File section > Verify Multiple File Upload

custom.preconds: The file upload section allows for multiple file uploads.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Select multiple files for upload (e.g., 2 or more files). 3. Click the 'Upload' button.
custom.expected: - All selected files are uploaded successfully. - Each file is listed in the uploaded section.

case.title: File section > Verify File Preview

custom.preconds: File upload section supports file preview.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Upload a file (e.g., PDF, image). 3. Check if a preview of the uploaded file is visible.
custom.expected: - The file preview is visible (e.g., an image thumbnail or document preview) once the file is uploaded. - The file preview should match the uploaded file.

case.title: File section > Verify File Deletion Functionality

custom.preconds: At least one file is uploaded in the file section.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Upload a file. 3. Delete the uploaded file by clicking the 'Delete' or 'Remove' button next to the file.
custom.expected: - The uploaded file is removed from the file section. - The file is no longer listed as uploaded.

case.title: File section > Verify Configuration Price Update with File Upload

custom.preconds: File upload is linked to a price change in the configurable product (e.g., uploading a custom logo adds to the price).
custom.steps: 1. Select a configuration for the product. 2. Upload a file (e.g., logo for customization). 3. Check if the price of the product updates according to the file uploaded.
custom.expected: - The price should update based on the uploaded file if the configuration affects the product price.

case.title: File section > Verify File Section for Different User Roles

custom.preconds: User is logged in with different roles.
custom.steps: 1. Log in as an admin and navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Log in as a customer and navigate to the same page.
custom.expected: - Admin should have full access to the file upload and deletion features. - Customers should be able to upload files but may not have access to delete or edit files depending on their role.

case.title: File section > Verify Required File Uploads

custom.preconds: The file upload is a required step for order completion (e.g., custom order).
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Attempt to add the product to the cart without uploading a file. 3. Attempt to complete the checkout process.
custom.expected: - A validation error should be shown, indicating that a file must be uploaded before proceeding.

case.title: File section > Verify Large File Upload Handling

custom.preconds: The file upload section should have a size limit.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Attempt to upload a file close to or at the maximum allowed size. 3. Verify if the file uploads successfully.
custom.expected: - Large files within the limit should upload successfully. - An error should appear if the file exceeds the allowed size.

case.title: File section > Verify Network Interruption During File Upload

custom.preconds: User is on the configurable product page and uploads a file.
custom.steps: 1. Start uploading a file. 2. Disconnect from the internet midway. 3. Reconnect and check the file upload behavior.
custom.expected: - The site should handle network interruptions gracefully. - If supported, the file upload should resume upon reconnection or prompt the user to retry.

section.name: Adding Configurable Product to Cart with Different options

case.title: Add Product Without Options

custom.preconds: 1. A configurable product exists in the catalog. 2. The product is available for purchase and has sufficient stock.

step.content: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Check the None option is selected in all sections. 3. Click the Add to Cart button. 4. Navigate to the cart page.
step.expected: The product appears in the cart without any additional options and the price matches the base product price.

case.title: Add Product with One Option Selected

custom.preconds: 1. A configurable product exists in the catalog. 2. The product is available for purchase and has sufficient stock.

step.content: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Select only one product from first section 3. Leave other sections as None 4.Click the Add to Cart button 5. Navigate to the cart page
step.expected:
The product appears in the cart with the selected option, and the price reflects the base price plus the selected option's price.
---
![](index.php?/attachments/get/73036204-fd19-4bcb-bd8a-275c04bb19c8)

case.title: Add Product with Multiple Options Selected

custom.preconds: A configurable product exists in the catalog. The configurations contains several sections (options) The product is available for purchase and has sufficient stock.

step.content: 1. Navigate to the configurable product page. 2. Select the products from all sections 2. Click the Add to Cart button. 3. Navigate to the cart page.
step.expected: The product appears in the cart with all selected options, and the price reflects the base price plus the total cost of the selected options.

case.title: Add configuration to cart > The "Edit Configuration" page is accessible

custom.preconds: 1. User must be logged into the application. 2. Cart must contain a configurable product.

step.content: 1. Navigate to the Cart page. 2. Locate a configurable product in the cart. 3. Expand the configuration details section (e.g., "Components List"). Verify that all selected options (e.g., color, components, and quantity) are displayed accurately
step.expected: The cart displays the selected options for the configurable product under its configuration details

step.content: 1. Click on the Edit Configuration button for a configurable product. 2. Verify that the product details page is opened in the same tab. 3. Verify that the product details page displays the current configuration without showing that the product is already in the cart
step.expected:
- The product details page opens in the same tab and displays the current configuration for editing. The product is not flagged as "already in cart."
- The URL contains lineItemId: {baseUrl}/category_slug/product_slug?lineItemId={id}

case.title: Changes made in the configuration are saved

custom.preconds: 1. User must be logged into the application. 2. Cart must contain a configurable product. 3. Click on the Edit Configuration button for a configurable product.

step.content: 1. Make changes to the products configuration (e.g., change color, components). 2. Save the changes and return to the cart. 3. Verify the updated configuration details are reflected in the cart.
step.expected:
- The Price per item and Total price are updated based on the changes made
- Changes made to the configuration are saved and displayed correctly in the cart. Prices are updated based on the new configuration.

case.title: The Cancel button discards changes in "Edit Configuration"

custom.preconds: User must be logged into the application. Cart must contain a configurable product. Click on the Edit Configuration button for a configurable product.

step.content: 1. Make changes to the product's configuration. 2. Click the Cancel button. 3. Verify that all changes are discarded, and the fields revert to their original values.
step.expected: Clicking the Cancel button discards all unsaved changes.

case.title: Notification toast > toast notification appears when the product configuration is changed.

step.content: 1. Open the configurable product page. 2. Modify any configuration 3. Observe the UI after making changes.
step.expected:
- An "Blue"-colored toast notification appears with the message: "The product configuration has been changed. You can always save it later - by clicking Update cart' button." - The toast notification includes a *Save* button.
---
![](index.php?/attachments/get/8bc8a7de-bbcf-4534-9e53-76c81a95235b)

case.title: Notification toast > Click "Save" button

step.content: 1. Open the configurable product page. 2. Modify any configuration 3. When the toast notification appears, click the *Save* button in the toast. 4. Refresh or re-open the configuration page.
step.expected: Changes are saved successfully. The toast notification disappears after clicking *Save*. On re-opening the configuration page, the updated configuration is displayed.

case.title: Save Changes pop-up appears on unsaved changes

step.content: 1. Open the configurable product configuration page. 2. Modify any configuration 3. Attempt to navigate away.
step.expected: A 'Save Changes' pop-up appears prompting the user to save or discard changes.

case.title: Save Changes pop-up does not appear when no changes are made

step.content: 1. Open the configuration page 2. Do not modify any settings. 3. Attempt to navigate away.
step.expected: The 'Save Changes' pop-up does not appear.

case.title: Save changes when 'Yes' is selected

step.content: 1. Modify any configuration. 2. Trigger the pop-up by leaving the page. 3. In the pop-up, select Yes 4. Re-open the configuration page.
step.expected: Changes are saved, and the updated configuration is displayed.

case.title: Discard changes when 'NO' is selected

step.content: 1. Modify any configuration. 2. Trigger the pop-up by leaving the page. 3. In the pop-up, select NO. 4. Re-open the configuration page.
step.expected: Changes are discarded, and the original configuration is displayed.

case.title: Remain on the configuration page when 'Cancel' is selected

step.content: 1. Modify any configuration. 2. Trigger the pop-up by leaving the page. 3. In the pop-up, click on X button
step.expected: User remains on the configuration page, and changes remain unsaved.

case.title: Verify UI design of Save Changes pop-up

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up by leaving the configuration page after making changes.
step.expected: The pop-up matches approved design specifications, including positioning, colors, fonts, and buttons.

case.title: Verify content of Save Changes pop-up

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up.
step.expected:
The pop-up contains: - Title: Save Changes - Message: The product configuration has been changed. Do you want to save it? - Buttons: YES, NO, X
---
![](index.php?/attachments/get/073c3b40-487c-4c4d-a72e-bdb37f891ccd)

case.title: Highlight 'YES' as the primary action

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up.
step.expected: The 'Save' button is visually emphasized as the primary action.

case.title: Verify keyboard navigation in Save Changes pop-up

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up. 2. Use keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape).
step.expected: - Tab navigates through buttons. - Enter activates the focused button. - Escape closes the pop-up.

case.title: Behavior when refreshing the page with unsaved changes

step.content: 1. Modify any configuration. 2. Press the browser refresh button.
step.expected: The 'Save Changes' pop-up appears before refreshing.

case.title: Behavior when closing the browser tab/window with unsaved changes

step.content: 1. Modify any configuration. 2. Attempt to close the browser tab/window.
step.expected: The pop-up or a browser alert prompts the user to save changes.

case.title: Pop-up triggers after multiple edits

step.content: 1. Modify configuration. 2. Save changes. 3. Modify again. 4. Attempt to navigate away.
step.expected: The 'Save Changes' pop-up appears only for new unsaved changes.

case.title: Behavior when network is disconnected

step.content: 1. Modify configuration. 2. Turn off the network. 3. Trigger the pop-up and click YES.
step.expected:
The system displays an error message
---
![](index.php?/attachments/get/08f4aa18-4c4d-4b33-90c8-2d58c18dd03b)

case.title: Verify pop-up in multiple browser tabs

step.content: 1. Open the configuration page in two tabs. 2. Modify configuration in one tab. 3. Attempt to leave in the first tab.
step.expected: The 'Save Changes' pop-up appears only in the active tab with unsaved changes.

case.title: Verify responsiveness on different screen sizes

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up on small, medium, and large screens.
step.expected: The pop-up is responsive and properly displayed on all screen sizes.

case.title: Verify cross-browser compatibility

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.
step.expected: The pop-up works consistently across supported browsers.

case.title: Verify pop-up on mobile devices

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up using a mobile browser.
step.expected: The pop-up is responsive and fully functional on mobile devices.

case.title: Accessibility compliance of the pop-up

step.content: 1. Trigger the pop-up. 2. Use screen readers and keyboard navigation.
step.expected: - Content is accessible via screen readers. - Buttons are keyboard accessible. - Contrast meets accessibility standards.

case.title: Verify Cart Contains Unique Product Configurations

custom.preconds: Repeat Scenario 1, Scenario 2, and Scenario 3 to add products with different configurations to the cart.

step.content: 1. Navigate to the cart page
step.expected:
Verify that the cart contains all unique configurations as separate line items: Base product without options. Product with one option selected. Product with multiple options selected. Verify that the "Components list" for each configuration is accurate and reflects the selected options.

case.title: Create Your Own Configuration > Buy

custom.preconds: Configurable product exists with configurations.
custom.steps: 1. Open the category page. 2. Verify the 'Customize Product' button. 3. Open configuration options. 4. Add the configured product to the cart. 5. Complete the checkout
custom.expected:
1. Products with unique configurations are added as separate line items in the cart. 2. The "Components list" for each line item accurately reflects the selected options. 3. The price for each configuration is calculated correctly based on the base product price and selected options. 4. The quantity and subtotal for each configuration are accurate. 5. The cart subtotal is the sum of all line items.

section.name: Can not add product to the cart

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > price N/A or 0

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > widget __Price__ > set up the price as 0 or remove price

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the price and button.
step.expected: 1. The price is __updated__ = 0 2. The product is not displayed in catalog

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > can't be purchased

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Switch off toggle __Can be purchased__

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the button.
step.expected: 1. Inactive field and button with is shown 2. Product could not be added to the cart

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > visible

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Switch off toggle __Visible__

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the button.
step.expected: 1. The product is not displayed in catalog 2. Product could not be added to the cart

case.title: Can't add to cart > inactive button > out of stock > FFC 0

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system in stock 2. Product_1 - belong to the Category_1 3. There is a price assigned to the product. 4. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > widget __Fulfillment center__ > Set 0 for all available FFC

step.content: 1. Go to the store. 2. Go to the product from the second precondition. 3. Pay attention to the button.
step.expected: 1. Red label __Out is stock__ shown 2. Inactive field and button with is shown 3. Product could not be added to the cart
