section.name: + and - Add to Cart
section.description:
https://virtocommerce.atlassian.net/browse/VCST-3031 Available from vc-frontend 2.24.0
Setting for UI change: client-app/config/settings_data.json
"product_quantity_control": "stepper" or "product_quantity_control": "button"

case.title: UI Element Replacement

custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the product listing page. 2. Observe the area where the 'Add to cart' button used to be.
custom.expected: The 'Add to cart' button is replaced with a '-' button

case.title: Increment Quantity

custom.steps: 1. Click the '+' button next to a product.
custom.expected: The quantity increases by 1 each time the '+' button is clicked.

case.title: Decrement Quantity

custom.steps: 1. Click the '+' button to set quantity to 1 or more. 2. Click the '-' button.
custom.expected: The quantity decreases by 1 each time

case.title: Add to Cart Functionality

custom.steps: 1. Click the '+' button to set quantity to 1 or more. 2. Check the cart.
custom.expected: The product appears in the cart with the correct quantity.

case.title: Remove from Cart

custom.steps: 1. Set a product quantity to 1 or more. 2. Click the '-' button until the quantity is 0. 3. Check the cart.
custom.expected: The product is removed from the cart.

case.title: Quantity Persistence

custom.steps: 1. Set a product quantity to 2. 2. Navigate away and return, or refresh the page.
custom.expected: The quantity remains as 2.

case.title: Stock Limit

custom.steps: 1. Click the '+' button repeatedly.
custom.expected: The quantity stops increasing at the maximum available stock.

case.title: Accessibility

custom.steps: 1. Use Tab/Shift+Tab to focus on the controls. 2. Use Enter/Space to activate '+' and '-'.
custom.expected: Controls are accessible and labeled for screen readers.

case.title: Cart > Price Update

custom.steps: 1. Change the quantity using '+/-'. 2. Observe the cart total.
custom.expected: The total price reflects the correct quantity.

case.title: Quantity Input Field

custom.steps: 1. Enter a number directly into the quantity input field.
custom.expected: The input field accepts only valid numbers within allowed range.

case.title: Min-Max Quantity Validation

custom.steps: 1. Enter a quantity below the minimum allowed. 2. Enter a quantity above the maximum allowed.
custom.expected: The system prevents input below min and above max

case.title: Stock Validation

custom.steps: 1. Enter a quantity greater than available stock.
custom.expected: The system prevents input above stock

case.title: Pack Size Validation

custom.steps: 1. Enter a quantity not matching the pack size (e.g., pack of 6, enter 7).
custom.expected: The system prevents invalid pack sizes

case.title: Pack Size Display

custom.steps: 1. Observe the pack size information near the quantity input field.
custom.expected: Pack size is clearly displayed to the user.

case.title: Pack Size Enforcement

custom.steps: 1. Attempt to add a quantity not matching the pack size.
custom.expected: System enforces pack size multiples and shows error if not matched.

case.title: Variation Table UI

custom.steps: 1. Navigate to a product with a variation table. 2. Observe the quantity controls for each variation.
custom.expected: Each variation row has its own '+/-' and quantity input field.

case.title: Variation Table Quantity Input

custom.steps: 1. Enter a quantity for a variation directly in the input field.
custom.expected: The input field accepts only valid numbers within allowed range for that variation.

case.title: Variation Table Min-Max/Stock/Pack Validation

custom.steps: 1. Enter invalid quantities (below min, above max, above stock, invalid pack size) for a variation.
custom.expected: System prevents invalid input and shows appropriate error for each variation.

case.title: Variation List UI

custom.steps: 1. Navigate to a product with a variation list. 2. Observe the quantity controls for each variation.
custom.expected: Each variation in the list has its own '+/-' and quantity input field.

case.title: Variation List Quantity Input

custom.steps: 1. Enter a quantity for a variation directly in the input field.
custom.expected: The input field accepts only valid numbers within allowed range for that variation.

case.title: Variation List Min-Max/Stock/Pack Validation

custom.steps: 1. Enter invalid quantities (below min, above max, above stock, invalid pack size) for a variation.
custom.expected: System prevents invalid input and shows appropriate error for each variation.

case.title: Related Products > Check + / - component

custom.steps: 1. On a product page, locate the related products section. 2. Use '+/-' or input field to set quantity for a related product.
custom.expected: Related product can be added to cart with correct quantity and validation.

case.title: Quates > Check + / - component

custom.steps: 1. On a quote page, use '+/-' or input field to set quantity for a quoted product.
custom.expected: The quantity is changed with step +1 or according with pack size step

case.title: Compare > Check + / - component

custom.steps: 1. On the compare page, use '+/-' or input field to set quantity for a compared product.
custom.expected: Compared product can be added to cart with correct quantity and validation.

case.title: Quantity Input Field Validation

custom.steps: 1. Enter non-numeric or negative values in the quantity input field.
custom.expected: System prevents non-numeric or negative input

section.name: Validation (Desktop+Mobile) (Add from Category+ Add from Product page)

case.title: Add product > validation > boundary values NOT IMPLEMENTED FULLY

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1- Product_2 - exist in the system in the Category_1 2. Product_1 in stock 5 3. Product_2 in stock 5 4. User logged in to the store 5. Add Product_1-Product_2 (for example 1 item each) 6. Open the Checkout page

step.content: 1. Change the amount of the items for the: - Product_1 to 5 items (as all items in the stock) - Product_2 to 6 items (as exceeded items in the stock) 2. Click button Update 3. Check count in the field for the Product_1-Product_3 updated
step.expected: 1. Count in the field for the Product_1-Product_3 updated - Product_1: 5 in stock - Product_1: 5 in stock NOT IMPLEMENTED YET

case.title: Add product > validation > messages under input field NOT IMPLEMENTED YET

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1- Product_3 - exist in the system in the Category_1 2. Product_1 in stock 5 3. Product_2 in stock 5 4. Product_3 in stock 5 5. User logged in to the store 6. Add Product_1-Product_3 (for example 1 item each) 7. Open the Checkout page

step.content: 1. Change the amount of the items for the: - Product_1 to 0 items. - Product_2 to 5 items (as all items in the stock) - Product_3 to 6 items (as exceeded items in the stock) 2. Click button Update 3. Check message under the input field
step.expected: 1. Message under the input field shown next message: - Product_1: Error message - can not add 0 - Product_2: Already in the cart - Product_3: reduce the amount?

case.title: Add product > validation > input field

custom.preconds: 1. User logged in to the store 2. Product_1-Product_10 added to the cart. 3. Product_1-Product_10 in stock more than 10 items each

step.content: 1. Change the amount of the items for the: - Product_1: 0 - Product_2: 1 0 - Product_3: space10 - Product_4: 10space - Product_5: 01 - Product_6: any text - Product_7: 10text - Product_8: space - Product_9: copy and paste the amount (for example 5) - Product_10: special char, such “, _ - / # etc. - Product_11: copy and paste special char, such “, _ - / # etc. - Product_12: <> - Product_13: %%%/%%%
step.expected: 1. Can't enter such chars

case.title: Add Product > min order limit > not defined

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store 2. Product_1 is available in stock (for example 10 items) 3. Product_1 doesn't have minimum an order limitation (to check the settings go to Admin/BAckEnd > Catalog > Category > Product 1)

step.content: 1. Open any Category. 2. Choose Product_1 3. Click button Add to cart (do not enter numder to the field)
step.expected: 1. 1 product by default have been added to the cart by default.

step.content: 1. Check the Product_1 block 2. Pay attention to the input field and button
step.expected: 1. Field save the input as 1 2. Text under the field is next: "1 in cart" 3. Button title has changed as Update Cart

case.title: Add Product > min order limit > set up

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store 2. Product_1 is available in stock (for example 10 items) 3. Min order limitation set up as for example 5 (to set up settings go to Admin/BAckEnd > Catalog > Category > Product 1)

step.expected: 5 is displayed in the input field

step.content: 1. Enter less than 5
step.expected:
1. The validation informer is displayed:
Order from 5 to 10 item(s)
2. The Add to Cart button is disabled

step.expected: 5 products have been added to the cart by default.

step.content: 1. Check the Product_1 block 2. Pay attention to the input field and button
step.expected: 1. Field save the input as 5 2. Text under the field is next: "N in cart" 3. Button title has changed as Update Cart

step.expected: 1. Field save the input is empty 2. No Text under the field presented 3. Button title Add to Cart

step.expected:

Verify:
!! There is no "shipping cost" in the Order Summary

step.content: Click on Proceed to Checkout
step.expected:
1. The redirection on {domain-name}/checkout/billing
2. The next elements are displayed:
title *Billing*
***Steps***
Back to Cart (active link) - Billing (unclickable) - 2 Order review (unclickable) - 3 Completed (unclickable)
***Payment details***
Billing address
Please select a billing address (link)
Payment method
Select payment method with options: Bank card (Authorize.Net) and Manual
***Order Summary***
!! There is no "shipping cost" in the Order Summary
Purchase Order field
Button Place Order (disabled by default if an address is not selected yet)
![](index.php?/attachments/get/0a903a94-e92d-4b47-831e-e38ad399f360)

step.content: Click on the link "Select a billing address" Select address Select Payment Method (ex. Manual)
step.expected: 1. The billing address was added to the cart (pencil icon is displayed) 2. Payment method is selected 3. Review Order button activated

step.content: Click on Review Order
step.expected:
The new page opens with URL/checkout/review
The next elements are displayed:
Title ORDER REVIEW
***Steps***
Back to Cart (active link) - Billing (clickable) - 2 Order review (active, unclickable) - 3 Completed (unclickable)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Title PLEASE REVIEW YOUR ORDER
Sections with next elements (read-only):
Line-items grouping by Vendors
Billing address Payment method Order comment (optional) Accepted gifts (optional)
------------------------------------------------------------------------ Order Summary
!! There is no "shipping cost" in the Order Summary
Button Place Order (enable by default)

step.content: Click on Place order
step.expected:
The new page opens with URL/checkout/completed
The next elements are displayed:
title ORDER COMPLETED
***Text***
Order CO{0:yyMMdd}-{1:D5} has been successfully submitted. You will be notified via email about the order status change.
***Buttons***
- Show Order - Home Page

case.title: Discount displayed

custom.preconds: 1. Create Promotion_1 as Coupon on the BackEnd. 2. Create Promotion_2 as Promotion Condition without the Coupon on the BackEnd. 3. Add short description for the Promotion_1 and Promotion_2 (for example: "Promotion")

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Add some products to the cart to match the the Coupon condition. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code for promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows description for the Promotion_1 and discount related to this promotion_1.

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Activate the Promotion condition for the promotion_2 on the BackEnd. 3. Add some products to the cart to match the Coupon and Promotion conditions. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code for the promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows the description for the Promotion_1 and discount related to this promotion_1. 4. Discount line shows the description for the Promotion_2 and discount related to this promotion_2.

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Activate the Promotion condition for the promotion_2 on the BackEnd. 3. Add a long description for Promotion_2 on BackEnd (For example "Promotion will give a discount from the total of the cart if a registered client will buy the bolts.". 4. Add some products to the cart to match the Coupon and Promotion conditions. 5. Go to the cart. 6. Enter the coupon code for the promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 7. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows the description in 2 lines only for the Promotion_1 and discount related to this promotion_1. 4. Discount line shows the description in 2 lines only for the Promotion_2 and discount related to this promotion_2.

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Activate the Promotion condition for the promotion_2 on the BackEnd. 3. Remove description for the Promotion_1 and Promotion_2 on BackEnd. 4. Add some products to the cart to match the Coupon and Promotion conditions. 5. Go to the cart. 6. Enter the coupon code for the promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 7. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows the only discount related to this promotion_1 without description. 4. Discount line shows the only discount related to this promotion_2 without description.

case.title: Apply coupon

custom.preconds:
Create a promotion on the BackEnd. Add a coupon to the promotion.
The coupon applied. The cart total is recalculated taking into account promotion discount

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Add some products to the cart to match the promotion conditions. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. The cart total is recalculated taking into account promotion discount

step.content:
1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Add some products to the cart to match the promotion conditions. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**.
Enter wrong coupon in Promotion code field > Apply
step.expected:
System display error message
Total not changes

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Add some products to the cart to match the promotion conditions. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**. Click on Remove with coupon
step.expected: System was removed coupon from order. Total was recalculated.

case.title: Discount displayed

custom.preconds: 1. Create Promotion_1 as Coupon on the BackEnd. 2. Create Promotion_2 as Promotion Condition without the Coupon on the BackEnd. 3. Add short description for the Promotion_1 and Promotion_2 (for example: "Promotion")

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Add some products to the cart to match the the Coupon condition. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code for promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows description for the Promotion_1 and discount related to this promotion_1.

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Activate the Promotion condition for the promotion_2 on the BackEnd. 3. Add some products to the cart to match the Coupon and Promotion conditions. 3. Go to the cart. 4. Enter the coupon code for the promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 5. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows the description for the Promotion_1 and discount related to this promotion_1. 4. Discount line shows the description for the Promotion_2 and discount related to this promotion_2.

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Activate the Promotion condition for the promotion_2 on the BackEnd. 3. Add a long description for Promotion_2 on BackEnd (For example "Promotion will give a discount from the total of the cart if a registered client will buy the bolts.". 4. Add some products to the cart to match the Coupon and Promotion conditions. 5. Go to the cart. 6. Enter the coupon code for the promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 7. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows the description in 2 lines only for the Promotion_1 and discount related to this promotion_1. 4. Discount line shows the description in 2 lines only for the Promotion_2 and discount related to this promotion_2.

step.content: 1. Go to the B2B store. 2. Activate the Promotion condition for the promotion_2 on the BackEnd. 3. Remove description for the Promotion_1 and Promotion_2 on BackEnd. 4. Add some products to the cart to match the Coupon and Promotion conditions. 5. Go to the cart. 6. Enter the coupon code for the promotion_1 to the **Promotion code** input field. 7. Click **Apply**.
step.expected: 1. The coupon applied. 2. The cart total is recalculated taking into account the promotion discount. 3. Discount line shows the only discount related to this promotion_1 without description. 4. Discount line shows the only discount related to this promotion_2 without description.

case.title: Product's block > stock decreased while being in the cart

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system with the available amount of the item in stock (for example 15) 2. There is a price assigned to the product_1 (eg 1500) 3. Product_1 presented in the store 4. User_1 logged in store

step.content: 1. Add Product_1 to the cart (for example,1 item) 2. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > widget FFC 3. Decrease the stock for all available FFC less than 15 (for example 10) 4. Go to the cart. 5. Pay attention to the Product_1
step.expected: 1. Product_1 presented in the cart 2. Input field highlighted in red color 2. Message __10 items in stock__ appears 3. Button Place order inactive

case.title: Product's block > out of stock while being in the cart

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system with the available amount of the item in stock (for example 15) 2. There is a price assigned to the product_1 (eg 1500) 3. Product_1 presented in the store 4. User_1 logged in store

step.content: 1. Add Product_1 to the cart (for example,1 item) 2. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > widget __FFC__ 3. Remove all available stock 4. Go to the cart. 5. Pay attention to the Product_1
step.expected: 1. Product_1 exists on the checkout page, but without the added amount of the item 2. Message "out of stock" appears 3. Button Remove

case.title: Product's block > can't be purchased while being in the cart

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system with the available amount of the item in stock (for example 15) 2. User logged in to the store 3. There is a price assigned to the product_1 4. Toggle Can be purchased __turn on__ 5. User_1 logged in store

step.content: 1. Add product_1 to the cart 2. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Switch off toggle Can be purchased 3. Go to the cart 4. Pay attention to the Product_1
step.expected: 1. There is a notification, that product is not longer available for purchase

case.title: Product's block > product is not visible

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system with the available amount of the item in stock (for example 15) 2. User logged in to the store 3. There is a price assigned to the product_1 4. Toggle Visibilite __turn on__ 5. User_1 logged in store

step.content: 1. Add product_1 to the cart 2. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Switch off toggle Visibility 3. Go to the cart 4. Pay attention to the Product_1
step.expected: 1. There is a notification, that product is not longer available for purchase

case.title: Product's block > product deleted while being in the cart

custom.preconds: 1. Product_1 exists in the system with the available amount of the item in stock (for example 15) 2. There is a price assigned to the product_1 (eg 1500) 3. Product_1 presented in the store 4. User_1 logged in store

step.content: 1. Add product_1 to the cart 2. Go to Admin Environment > Catalog > Product_1 > Delete the product_1 from the Category 3. Go to the cart 4. Pay attention to the Product_1
step.expected: 1. There is a notification, that product is not longer available for purchase

case.title: Order summary > Total

section.name: Keep Cart Products if Change Currency
section.description:
The feature available from vc-frontend 2.12.0
https://virtocommerce.atlassian.net/browse/VCST-2454
Check mobile version!

case.title: Anonim USD Cart with products -> New customer without defaultCurrency and defaultLanguage

custom.preconds: Create a new customer

step.content: Add products to cart
step.expected: Anonymous USD cart contains products

step.content: 1. Sign in under a new customer 3. Check the cart
step.expected: There are product items (from anonim) are in cart

case.title: Anonim USD Cart is empty -> Customer default currency EUR

custom.preconds: User's default currency is EUR in Profile EUR cart contains products Anonymous USD cart is empty

step.content: Sign in as a customer Add products to cart EUR.
step.expected: The cart EUR contains products

step.content: 1. Log out 2. Sign in again 3. Check the cart in EUR.
step.expected: The User sees items from EUR cart Nothing removed

case.title: Anonim USD Cart contains items -> Registered User Cart with EUR as default currency

custom.preconds: User's default currency is EUR in Profile USD cart contains products EUR cart contains products

step.content: 1. Go to frontend as a Anonyms user. 2. Add products to the USD cart. 3. Sign in as a registered user with EUR cart. 4. Check the cart in EUR.
step.expected:
Customer sees products only from Anonymous cart USD! The old items from EUR cart were removed!
Products without EUR prices are displayed with a price of 0. A user-friendly message is shown:
The product is not longer available for purchase.

case.title: User updated default currency - > Check products in cart

custom.preconds: Product has only USD price Default user currency is USD USD cart contains Products

step.content: Go to Profile Set default currency as GBP, British pound Update
step.expected: The default currency is updated

step.content: Check the GBP cart
step.expected: Existing GBP cart cleared, products moved from USD to GBP cart Quantities remain the same. Products display correct prices in British pound. Quantities remain the same. Validation rules are applied.

case.title: Anonymous Cart USD, Customer Cart USD, Default Customer Currency USD

custom.preconds: Anonymous has items in USD cart Default Customer Currency USD Customer has items in USD cart

step.content: Go to store as Anonymous Add products to cart
step.expected: The Anonymous cart USD contains items

step.content: Sign in as a customer Check cart
step.expected: The customer sees the items from both carts. Anonymous items are merged with customer items

case.title: Anonymous Cart USD, Customer Cart USD, Default Customer Currency EUR

custom.preconds: Anonymous has items in USD cart Default Customer Currency EUR Customer EUR cart contains items

step.content: Log in as a customer Open cart
step.expected: The customer sees products in EUR

step.content: Set USD as a store currency
step.expected:
All products moved to USD cart No duplicate products or unexpected items appear.
Products without USD prices are displayed with a price of 0. A user-friendly message is shown:
The product is not longer available for purchase.

step.content: Log out
step.expected: The Cart is empty

step.content: Go to catalog as Anonymous Add products to cart USD
step.expected: The Anonymous cart USD contains items

step.content: Log in as a customer Check Default Customer Currency is EUR Check cart EUR
step.expected:
Customer sees products only from Anonymous cart USD! The old items from EUR cart were removed!
Products without EUR prices are displayed with a price of 0. A user-friendly message is shown:
The product is not longer available for purchase.

case.title: Customer Cart USD, Default Customer Currency EUR

custom.preconds: Default Customer Currency EUR Customer EUR cart contains different product types (configurable products with text section type, digital products and etc.)

step.content: Log in as a customer Open cart
step.expected: The customer sees products in EUR

step.content: Set USD as a store currency
step.expected:
All products moved to USD cart
Products without USD prices are displayed with a price of 0. A user-friendly message is shown:
The product is not longer available for purchase.

step.content: Log out
step.expected: The Cart is empty

step.content: Log in as a customer Check Default Customer Currency is EUR Check cart EUR
step.expected: Customer sees products from EUR cart

case.title: Price exists for this currency -> USD and EUR carts contain products

custom.preconds: Products have both prices in USD and EUR Default currency is USD in Store USD cart contains products EUR cart contains products

step.content: 1. Add products to the cart in USD. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Check the contents of the EUR cart.
step.expected: Existing Euro cart cleared, products moved from USD to EUR cart Products display correct prices in EUR. Quantities remain the same. Validation rules are applied.

case.title: Price exists for this currency -> USD cart contains Configurable products

custom.preconds: !!! Configurable Product and options have both prices in USD and EUR Default currency is USD in Store USD cart contains products EUR cart contains products

step.content: 1. Add products to the cart in USD. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Check the contents of the EUR cart.
step.expected: - Existing Euro cart cleared - Configurable product with all options moved from USD to EUR cart - Products display correct prices in EUR. - Quantities remain the same. - Validation rules are applied.

case.title: Price exists for this currency -> USD cart contains products and EUR cart is empty

custom.preconds: Products have both prices in USD and EUR Default currency is USD in Store USD cart contains products EUR cart is empty

step.content: 1. Add products to the cart in USD. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Check the contents of the EUR cart.
step.expected: All products moved from USD to EUR cart Products display correct prices in EUR. Quantities remain the same. Validation rules are applied.

case.title: Price does not exist for this currency -> USD and EUR carts contain products

custom.preconds: Product have price only in USD Default currency is USD in Store USD cart contains products EUR cart contains products

step.content: 1. Add products to the cart in USD where items have no EUR price. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Observe the EUR cart contents.
step.expected:
Products without EUR prices are displayed with a price of 0. A user-friendly message is shown:
The product is not longer available for purchase.

case.title: Some products have EURO price and some does not have -> USD to EUR

custom.preconds:
Product1 have price in USD and EUR Product2 have price only in USD
Default currency is USD in Store USD cart contains products EUR cart contains products

step.content: 1. Add products to the cart in USD where some items have no EUR price. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Observe the EUR cart contents.
step.expected: Products display correct prices in EUR. Quantities remain the same. Products without EUR prices are displayed with a price of 0. A user-friendly message is shown: The product is not longer available for purchase.

case.title: Validate Smart Merge Failure

step.content: 1. Add products to the USD cart. 2. Simulate a failure in the Smart Merge. 3. Switch the currency to EUR. 4. Check the EUR cart contents.
step.expected: The EUR cart remains empty. A clear error message is displayed. The USD cart remains unaffected.

case.title: Validate Cart Switching Back - EUR to USD

step.content: 1. Add products to the cart in USD. 2. Switch the currency to EUR and verify the cart. 3. Switch back to USD. 4. Observe the cart contents.
step.expected: The original USD cart is restored. No duplicate items or inconsistencies occur. Prices are correctly displayed in USD.

case.title: Verify Quantity Consistency During Currency Switch

custom.preconds: Product1 have both prices for USD and EUR Default store currency is USD

step.content: 1. Add a product with quantity = 3 to the USD cart. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Check the product quantity in the EUR cart.
step.expected: Product quantity remains 3. Prices are updated to EUR. No additional line items are created.

case.title: Verify Price Recalculation After Switching Currency

step.content: 1. Add products with USD prices to the cart. 2. Switch the currency to EUR. 3. Verify the recalculated product prices and totals.
step.expected: Prices in the EUR cart match the corresponding EUR PriceList. Totals are recalculated accurately. No mismatches or rounding errors occur.

case.title: Verify Behavior When EUR PriceList Does Not Exist

step.content: 1. Add products with USD prices to the cart. 2. Ensure no EUR PriceList exists for these products. 3. Switch the currency to EUR. 4. Check the EUR cart.
step.expected: Products are shown with a 0 EUR price or a clear unavailable message. No crashes or freezes occur.

case.title: Validate Switch Currency with Mixed Product Variants

step.content: 1. Add products with multiple variants to the USD cart. 2. Ensure only some variants have EUR prices. 3. Switch the currency to EUR. 4. Verify the cart behavior.
step.expected: Variants with EUR prices are displayed correctly. Variants without EUR prices are shown with 0 EUR or an unavailable message. Quantities remain consistent.

case.title: Add Product > merge cart > anonymously then login

step.expected: 1. Product_1 added to the cart in amount of 2 items

step.expected: 1. Next information is shown: Cart (2)

step.content: 1. Login to the store as User_1 2. Сheck the cart
step.expected: 1. Product_1 added to the cart in amount of 2 items 2. Count on Menu near Cart is shown: Cart (2)

step.expected: 1. Product_1 added to the cart in amount of 2 items

step.content: 1. Check count on Menu near Cart link
step.expected: 1. Next information is shown: Cart (2)

step.content: 1. Register User_1 2. Login as User_1 3. Сheck the cart
step.expected: 1. Product_1 added to the cart in amount of 2 items 2. Count on Menu near Cart is shown: Cart (2)

custom.preconds: 1. User_1 logged in store 2. Product_1 is available in stock (for example 10 items)

step.content: 1. Open any Category. 2. Choose Product_1 3. Enter N to the field near the button Add to cart (for example 2) 4. Click Add to cart. 5. Check count on Menu near Cart link
step.expected: 1. Next information is shown Cart (2)

case.title: Add Product > Anonymously > Menu Cart count

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the store anonymously 2. Product_1 is available in stock (for example 10 items)

step.content: 1. Open any Category. 2. Choose Product_1 3. Enter N to the field near the button Add to cart (for example 2) 4. Click Add to cart. 5. Check count on Menu near Cart link
step.expected: 1. Next information is shown Cart (2)

step.expected: 1. Next information shown Cart (4)

case.title: Cart Merge After Login (Anonymous Cart + User Cart)

custom.preconds: Anonymous user has products in the cart (AnonymousCart). Logged-in user already has products in their cart (UserCart).
custom.steps: 1. Add products to the cart as an anonymous user. 2. Log in with a registered customer account. 3. Open the cart.
custom.expected: The cart should contain both products from AnonymousCart and UserCart. No products should be lost or duplicated.

case.title: Merging Logic - No Duplicates

custom.preconds: Same product exists in both AnonymousCart and UserCart.
custom.steps: 1. Add Product A (Qty: 1) to the cart as an anonymous user. 2. Log in as a customer (who also has Product A (Qty: 2) in their cart). 3. Open the cart.
custom.expected: The merged cart should show Product A (Qty: 3) (sum of both carts). No duplicate products should appear.

case.title: Cart Merge When Store Currency Changes

custom.preconds: AnonymousCart contains products in USD. UserCart contains products in EUR (default customer currency).
custom.steps: 1. As an anonymous user, add products in USD. 2. Log in as a user with a cart in EUR. 3. Check the cart contents.
custom.expected: All products should merge into a single currency cart. If any product is unavailable in the new currency, it should be displayed at price 0 with a warning message: 'This product is no longer available for purchase.'

case.title: Cart Merge When User Logs Out & Logs In Again

custom.preconds: Anonymous user adds products to AnonymousCart. The user logs in (cart merges).
custom.steps: 1. Log in and verify the merged cart. 2. Log out. 3. Log in again with the same user account. 4. Check the cart contents.
custom.expected: The merged cart should persist after logout. No products should be lost or duplicated.

case.title: Cart Merge with Different Product Types

custom.preconds: AnonymousCart contains simple products. UserCart contains configurable/bundle products.
custom.steps: 1. Add different product types to AnonymousCart and UserCart. 2. Log in and open the cart.
custom.expected: The merged cart should contain all products with correct attributes (size, color, etc.). No missing or mismatched products should appear.

The symbols counter starts to increase in real-time and shows the quantity of entered symbols (Ex. 10/1000).

step.expected: The symbols counter increased and show the quantity of entered symbols (1000/1000). The new comment is added and saved in the Comments.

step.expected: The counter shows symbols count 1000/1000. The field limit entered text to a 1000 chars

step.expected: Symbols counter decrease and show 0/1000.

section.name: Delivery option: select address for checkout

case.title: Prefill Pickup Address from Local Storage for Anonymous User

custom.preconds: Not logged in; local storage enabled; Pickup address previously entered

step.content: 1. Select Pickup. 2. Enter address, continue/save. 3. Refresh/revisit, select Pickup.
step.expected: Address is prefilled from local storage.

case.title: Fixed Rate Address Not Prefilled if Local Storage Cleared

custom.preconds: Not logged in; local storage was used, but now cleared

step.content: 1. Go to checkout. 2. Select Fixed Rate (Ground) shipping method.
step.expected: Address field is empty.

case.title: Pickup Address Not Saved if Local Storage Disabled

custom.preconds: Not logged in; browser local storage is disabled

step.content: 1. Enter Pickup address. 2. Complete/leave and return. 3. Select Pickup.
step.expected: Address field is empty.

case.title: Address Not Prefilled if Data is Corrupted

custom.preconds: Corrupted/invalid address for method in storage

step.content: 1. Select affected shipping method.
step.expected: Address field remains empty or shows validation error.

case.title: Prefill Long/Special Character Address

custom.preconds: Last saved address contains maximum length and special characters

step.content: 1. Select relevant shipping method.
step.expected: Address prefilled correctly, no truncation or error.

case.title: Rapid Switch: Address Prefill Always Correct

custom.preconds: Multiple addresses saved for Pickup and both Fixed Rates

step.content: 1. Rapidly switch between Pickup, Fixed Rate (Ground), Fixed Rate (Air).
step.expected: Address field updates instantly and correctly for each selection.

case.title: Anonymous: Prefill Isolated Across Browsers

custom.preconds: Two anonymous users on different browsers, each saved different Pickup address

step.content: 1. Save different Pickup addresses in each. 2. Refresh/revisit both.
step.expected: Each session shows only its own Pickup address.

case.title: Prefill Pickup Address for Personal User

custom.preconds: User is logged in; previously used Pickup with Address A

step.content: 1. Go to checkout. 2. Select Pickup shipping method.
step.expected: Address field is prefilled with last used Pickup address (Address A).

case.title: Prefill Fixed Rate (Ground) Address for Personal User

custom.preconds: User is logged in; previously used Fixed Rate (Ground) with Address B

step.content: 1. Go to checkout. 2. Select Fixed Rate (Ground) shipping method.
step.expected: Address field is prefilled with last used address for Fixed Rate (Ground) (Address B).

case.title: No Prefill if No Previous Address for Fixed Rate (Air)

custom.preconds: User is logged in; never used Fixed Rate (Air) before

step.content: 1. Go to checkout. 2. Select Fixed Rate (Air) shipping method.
step.expected: Address field is empty.

case.title: Change and Save New Address for Pickup

custom.preconds: User is logged in; on checkout, Pickup selected

step.content: 1. Enter a new Pickup address. 2. Complete/save. 3. Revisit checkout and select Pickup.
step.expected: New Pickup address is prefilled.

case.title: Switch Between Pickup and Fixed Rate Addresses

custom.preconds: User is logged in; has previously saved addresses for Pickup and Fixed Rate

step.content: 1. Select Pickup (see Pickup address). 2. Switch to Fixed Rate. 3. Switch back to Pickup.
step.expected: Each method’s address field updates to correct saved address.

case.title: Prefill Pickup Address for Organization Account

custom.preconds: User is logged in to organization; Pickup used before; Org Pickup Address X

step.content: 1. Go to checkout as org user. 2. Select Pickup shipping method.
step.expected: Address field is prefilled with org’s Pickup address.

case.title: Organization Policy Restricts Address Storage (Fixed Rate)

custom.preconds: Org policy does not allow address saving

step.content: 1. Select Fixed Rate shipping method. 2. Enter address. 3. Save and revisit checkout.
step.expected: Address field remains empty; not prefilled.

case.title: Org User Switches Between Organizations for Pickup

custom.preconds: User is member of Org1 (Pickup address Y) and Org2 (Pickup address Z)

step.content: 1. Login as Org1, select Pickup. 2. Switch to Org2, select Pickup.
step.expected: Address prefilled with Pickup address specific to each org.

case.title: Address Save Blocked by Access Rights

custom.preconds: Org user role does not permit address saving

step.content: 1. Attempt to save address for Pickup or Fixed Rate.
step.expected: Address is not saved; on revisit, address field is empty or error shown.

case.title: Validate Payment with an Expired Card

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a valid Visa card number. 2. Enter a valid cardholder name. 3. Enter an expired expiration date (MM/YY format). 4. Enter a valid CVV. 5. Click on the 'PAY NOW' button.
custom.expected: The system displays an error message: 'Card has expired.' The payment is not processed.

case.title: Validate Payment with an Incorrect CVV

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a valid Visa card number. 2. Enter a valid cardholder name. 3. Enter a valid expiration date. 4. Enter an invalid CVV (less than 3 digits). 5. Click on the 'PAY NOW' button.
custom.expected: The system displays an error message: 'Invalid CVV. Please enter a 3-digit code.' The payment is not processed.

case.title: Validate that the 'PAY NOW' Button is Disabled When Fields are Empty

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Leave all fields empty. 2. Observe the 'PAY NOW' button.
custom.expected: The 'PAY NOW' button remains disabled.

case.title: Validate Minimum Length for Card Number

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a single digit in the 'Card Number' field. 2. Observe the validation message displayed below the field.
custom.expected: A validation message should appear stating that the 'Card Number must be at least 12 characters'.

case.title: Validate Minimum Length for Security Code

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a single character in the 'Security Code' field. 2. Observe the validation message displayed below the field.
custom.expected: A validation message should appear stating that 'Security code must be at least 3 characters'.

case.title: Validate Expiration Date Format

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a single digit in the 'Expiration Date' field. 2. Observe the validation message displayed below the field.
custom.expected: A validation message should appear stating that 'Month must be exactly 2 characters'.

case.title: Validate Cardholder Name Minimum Length

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a single character in the 'Cardholder Name' field. 2. Observe the validation message (if any) displayed below the field.
custom.expected: System should validate if the name length is acceptable, and show an error if a single character is not allowed.

case.title: Validate Error Message for an Invalid Card Number

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Enter an invalid card number. 2. Enter a valid cardholder name. 3. Enter a valid expiration date. 4. Enter a valid CVV. 5. Click on the 'PAY NOW' button.
custom.expected: The system displays an error message: 'Invalid card number.' The payment is not processed.

case.title: Validate Keyboard Navigation in Payment Form

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page.
custom.steps: 1. Use the 'Tab' key to navigate through the fields. 2. Verify that the focus moves correctly from -Card Number -Cardholder Name -Expiration Date -Security Code -PAY NOW button 3. Press Shift + Tab and verify that the focus moves in reverse order.
custom.expected: The keyboard navigation works as expected, and users can navigate through fields without using a mouse.

case.title: Validate Clear Input Functionality

custom.preconds: User is on the payment page with some input entered in the form fields.
custom.steps: 1. Enter a valid card number, cardholder name, expiration date, and security code. 2. Use the 'Backspace' key or 'Delete' key to clear each field. 3. Observe whether the fields are cleared correctly. 4. Click outside the field to check if the placeholder text reappears.
custom.expected: The input fields should be cleared when using 'Backspace' or 'Delete'. Placeholder text should reappear when the field is empty.

case.title: Verify Payment Methods List in Store

custom.preconds:
Install Payment Module - Ex: VirtoCommerce.AuthorizeNetPayment.
Please enable it for the store.
Create an order with VirtoCommerce.AuthorizeNetPayment and don’t pay.
Create a cart with VirtoCommerce.AuthorizeNetPayment and don’t create an order.
First Scenarios - !!! Don’t deactivate the Payment method from the Store and Uninstall the Payment Module.
Second Scenarios - !!! Just Deactivate the payment.
custom.steps: 1. Navigate to the Store settings. 2. Open the Payment Methods blade.
custom.expected: No errors should be displayed. The list should contain only installed and active payment methods. Uninstalled payment methods should not be listed.

case.title: Verify Order Payment Details in Backend

custom.preconds: Create an order using any Payment provider but do not complete the payment.
custom.steps: 1. Open the created order. 2. Open the associated payment document.
custom.expected: No errors should be displayed. Payment details should be visible and correctly associated with the order.

case.title: Verify Checkout Payment Methods

custom.preconds: Create a cart with any Payment method but do not place an order.
custom.steps: 1. Open the cart. 2. Proceed to checkout. 3. Navigate to the payment section.
custom.expected: No errors should be displayed. Only installed and active payment methods should be listed.

case.title: Checkout Payment Methods > Uninstall module

custom.preconds: Create a cart with any Payment method but do not place an order.

step.content: 1. Open the cart. 2. Proceed to checkout. 3. Navigate to the payment section. 4. Select Payment method 5. Go to backend uninstall Payment module 6. Try to create a order
step.expected: The error message should be displayed. The Payment Failed The Error when creating an order Redirecting to /cart page

step.content: 1. Open the cart page 2. Proceed to checkout. 3. Navigate to the payment section. 4. Select Payment method
step.expected: The errors should be displayed. The Proceed button should be disabled. Only installed and active payment methods should be listed.

case.title: Verify Order Payment Process

custom.preconds: Create an order using any Payment method but do not pay. Do not deactivate or uninstall the payment method.
custom.steps: 1. Open the order. 2. Click the 'Pay' button.
custom.expected: No errors should be displayed. The order should exist, and the payment method field (paymentMethod) should be null. initializePayment should not be triggered. The 'Edit' button should allow changing the payment method.

case.title: Verify Order Payment When Payment Method is Deactivated

custom.preconds: Deactivate the Payment method for the store. Reset the cache if required. Do not uninstall the payment module.
custom.steps: 1. Open an existing order that used any Payment provider 2. Click the 'Pay' button.
custom.expected: No errors should be displayed. initializePayment should return: {"isSuccess": false, "errorMessage": "Payment method {PaymentMethodName} is inactive"}. The 'Edit' button should allow changing the payment method.

custom.preconds: 1. Go to the cart. 2. Add at least 10 products to the cart 3. Proceed to checkout.

Next blocks are presented: - My purchased products - Accepted gifts with amount - Order comment - Order summary - Shipping address (expandable) - Shipping method (expandable) - Billing address (expandable) - Payment method (expandable) - Button Print Order - Button Pay now

step.expected: 1. Block includes the following information: - Product lines - Quantity - Total

step.content: 1. Check the amount of the product presented in the list
step.expected: 1. 8 products presented 2. Pagination presented 2 pages

step.content: 1. Check Order summary block
step.expected: 1. Next elements are presented: - Purchased order title - Purchased order field and submit button - Promotion order title - Promotion order field and submit button - Subtotal - Discount (expandable) - Tax - Shipping cost - Total

step.content: 1. Check the next block: - Billing address - Shipping method - Payment address - Payment method
step.expected:
1. This block contains next information: - Shipping address: First and Last name, Line2, Line1, City, Region, Country, ZIP, phone, email - Shipping method: - Billing address: - Payment method: First and Last name, Line2, Line1, City, Region, Country, ZIP, phone, email
First and Last name, Line2, Line1, City, Region, Country, ZIP, phone, email

Verify Review List Displays Correctly

step.content: Navigate to the 'Reviews' section and observe the review list.
step.expected: All existing reviews are displayed with details like Author, Rating, Status, Date. Paginated if reviews exceed the page limit.

case.title: Verify Review Sorting Functionality

step.content: Sort the review list by Date, Rating, and Status.
step.expected: Reviews are sorted accurately according to the selected criteria.

case.title: Verify Filtering of Reviews

step.content: Filter reviews by rating and status, then clear filters.
step.expected: Only reviews matching the selected filter are displayed; clearing filters shows the full list.

case.title: Verify Viewing Review Details

step.content: Select a review from the list to view details.
step.expected: Review details (Author, Rating, Date, Content, Status) are displayed correctly.

case.title: Verify Changing Review Status

step.content: Change the status of a review to 'Approved,' 'Rejected,' and 'Pending.'
step.expected: The status updates successfully and reflects in the review list.

case.title: Verify Deleting a Review

step.content: Select and delete a review.
step.expected: The review is deleted and no longer appears in the list.

case.title: Verify Pagination Functionality

step.content: Navigate between pages in the review list.
step.expected: Reviews are displayed according to the selected page.

case.title: Verify Search Functionality for Reviews

step.content: Search for reviews by keyword (e.g., author name).
step.expected: Only reviews matching the search keyword are displayed.

case.title: Verify Bulk Actions for Reviews

step.content: Select multiple reviews and apply bulk actions (e.g., approve all).
step.expected: Bulk action is applied to all selected reviews successfully.

case.title: Verify Behavior When No Reviews Exist

step.content: Navigate to the 'Reviews' section when no reviews are present.
step.expected: A message such as 'No reviews available' is displayed. The UI remains functional.

case.title: Verify Adding a Review with Invalid Data

step.content: Attempt to submit a review with invalid or malicious data.
step.expected: System rejects invalid input with appropriate error message. No malicious content is stored or executed.

case.title: Verify Maximum Characters for Review Content

step.content: Submit reviews at and beyond the character limit.
step.expected: Reviews within limit are accepted; exceeding limit are rejected with a clear error message.

case.title: Verify Filtering or Sorting with No Matching Results

step.content: Apply filters or sorting criteria that yield no results.
step.expected: Message like 'No reviews match the criteria' is displayed. System remains stable.

case.title: Verify Deleting All Reviews

step.content: Select all reviews and delete them.
step.expected: All selected reviews are deleted successfully. System reflects an empty state with an appropriate message.

case.title: Verify Concurrent Actions on a Single Review

step.content: Simulate two users updating/deleting the same review simultaneously.
step.expected: One action succeeds, the other is rejected with an appropriate message.

case.title: Verify Handling of Long Review Content

step.content: View reviews with exceptionally long content.
step.expected: UI remains responsive; content is truncated or scrollable.

case.title: Verify System Behavior During a Timeout

step.content: Simulate a timeout during review management actions.
step.expected: Clear error message like 'Request timed out. Please try again.' System remains stable.

case.title: Verify Behavior When Review Data is Corrupted

step.content: Simulate corrupted review data in the database.
step.expected: System displays error for corrupted data but remains functional. Other reviews are unaffected.

case.title: Verify Unauthorized Access to Review Management

step.content: Log in as a user without permissions and attempt to access 'Reviews' section.
step.expected: Access denied with message like 'You do not have permission to view this page.'

case.title: Verify Moderation Status Change to 'Approved'

step.content: Navigate to the 'Reviews' section, select a review with 'Pending' or 'Rejected' status, and change its status to 'Approved.'
step.expected: The review's status updates to 'Approved' and is reflected in the list.

case.title: Verify Moderation Status Change to 'Rejected'

step.content: Navigate to the 'Reviews' section, select a review with 'Pending' or 'Approved' status, and change its status to 'Rejected.'
step.expected: The review's status updates to 'Rejected' and is reflected in the list.

case.title: Verify Bulk Moderation of Reviews

step.content: Select multiple reviews in 'Pending' or 'Rejected' status and use bulk actions to set them to 'Approved.'
step.expected: All selected reviews' statuses update to 'Approved.'

case.title: Verify Moderation Actions by Unauthorized Users

step.content: Log in with a user account without moderation permissions and attempt to change a review's status.
step.expected: Action is denied with an appropriate error message like 'You do not have permission to perform this action.'

case.title: Verify Moderation of Reviews with Invalid Status

step.content: Attempt to set a review's status to an invalid value (e.g., 'UnknownStatus').
step.expected: System rejects the invalid status update with an error and retains the original status.

case.title: Verify Moderation Workflow for Reviews with Missing Fields

step.content: Navigate to the 'Reviews' section and attempt to moderate a review with incomplete or corrupted data.
step.expected: System displays an appropriate error or warning. Moderation is logged correctly or prevented.

case.title: Verify Simultaneous Moderation Actions

step.content: Simulate two users moderating the same review simultaneously (one approves, the other rejects).
step.expected: The first action succeeds, and the second user receives an error or notification about the updated review status.

case.title: Verify Moderation History Logging

step.content: Change a review's status to 'Approved' or 'Rejected' and check system logs or history.
step.expected: The action is logged with details such as user, date/time, and previous/new statuses.

case.title: Verify Email Notification Triggered on Review Approval

step.content: Approve a review and check the review author's email inbox.
step.expected: An email notification is sent to the review author about the approval.

case.title: Verify System Behavior When Moderation Service is Unavailable

step.content: Simulate downtime for the moderation service and attempt to moderate a review.
step.expected: System displays an error like 'Unable to process the moderation request' and allows retrying later.
